You’ll get this at the end of each pay period, along with your normal pay and it’s one of the best perks about working in hospitality.

title: Commercial Analyst
description: **Tech that takes you places** We’re CarTrawler, the global travel tech company behind seamless connections between people, places and possibilities. From powering car rental to creating smarter ways to move, we make travel smoother for millions worldwide. Our culture is built on curiosity, collaboration and craic where every idea counts and every journey matters. Ready to make an impact? Let’s go places together. The Commercial Analyst plays a critical role in supporting the Head of P\&L, Commercial Manager and Trading Squad in making informed business decisions. Working within a cross\-functional, self\-organised Trading Squad, the Commercial Analyst will drive insights, analyse performance, and communicate key trends to stakeholders. The role requires strong commercial acumen and the ability to identify, prioritise, and act upon opportunities for growth, focusing on driving profitable outcomes across multiple partners and markets. The Commercial Analyst will also collaborate across the wider Trading Analyst Group, ensuring that learnings are captured, shared, and implemented across the organisation. Additionally, the role will involve project work and providing analytical support for various initiatives, ensuring that data\-driven strategies are consistently aligned with the organisation's broader goals. **Your Journey starts here!** **What you’ll do as a Commercial Analyst;** **Performance Analysis \& Communication:** * Analyse trading performance and identify the key drivers influencing outcomes. * Communicate findings to stakeholders in a clear, tailored way to ensure insights are easily understood and actionable. **Project Leadership \& Co\-management:** * Support ad\-hoc and long\-term projects, such as partner performance optimisation and strategic initiatives, by providing data insights and recommendations. **P\&L Deep Dives:** * Conduct regular financial P\&L deep dives on selected partner groups, providing actionable insights for performance improvement. **Reporting Framework Development:** * Develop scalable, automated reporting that allow channel and P\&L owners to analyse performance and identify trends effectively. **P\&L Performance Communication:** * Ensure that performance trends related to pricing, digital merchandising, content, and conversion are well communicated, contextualised, and prioritised to drive business growth. **Collaboration and Action:** * Work closely with the Trading Squad to identify growth opportunities and ensure these are developed and executed across the business. * Collaborate with other teams (e.g., business, product, finance) to ensure commercial strategies are aligned and backed by robust analytical insights. **Project Support:** * Provide analytical support for cross\-functional projects, offering insights from performance data to shape strategy and ensure alignment with business goals. * Contribute to special initiatives, such as pricing forums, new product launches, or partner onboarding, by supporting the data analysis and performance tracking. **What You’ll Bring To The Team** * At least 3\+ years in a similar role, with a proven track record in data analysis, identifying commercial opportunities, and driving business results. * A third\-level degree in IT, Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, or a related field. * Strong experience working with large datasets and practical experience with SQL. * Expertise in data visualisation, including creating interactive dashboards with tools such as Tableau or ThoughtSpot. * A passion for data and the ability to use it to identify commercial opportunities and product improvements. * A strong understanding of Excel and PowerPoint are essential. * Ability to clearly and succinctly communicate complex findings to a non\-specialist audience. * Strong collaboration and communication skills, working effectively across teams and with external partners **Nice to Have:** * Statistical Programming: * Experience with statistical programming languages like R or Python is a plus, though not essential. **Go on, give it a go!** We know that looking for a new job can feel a bit daunting but don’t let a touch of imposter syndrome hold you back. Even if you don’t tick every box, we’d still love to hear from you! Apply today to stay connected. At CarTrawler, we’re proud to be an equal opportunities employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for everyone. No matter your background, identity, or life experience, if you’re passionate about what you do, you’ll be welcomed here. **Perks for the journey** * Competitive pay and benefits, including a performance\-based annual bonus, fully subsidised VHI health insurance and pension matching up to 4% * Generous time off and flexible working, including 25 days’ annual leave, extra company days, monthly Friday Unplugged afternoons and the option to work abroad for up to 20 days a year * Comprehensive wellbeing support, including access to health and mental health programmes (HeadsUp and EAP) and paid leave for marriage, volunteering and personal wellbeing including menstrual, menopause, and fertility leave * Growth and recognition culture with development opportunities through training, coaching and study support plus programmes that celebrate individual and team achievements * Everyday perks that make a difference \- subsidised canteen (KC Peaches), on\-site car parking, Bike to Work and TaxSaver schemes plus an active Sports \& Social Club We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Commercial Finance Analyst - Newly Qual ACA
description: This commercial finance position represents a superb opportunity for a recently qualified ACA to join the organisation at an exciting time of their business growth and development. The Finance Business Partner will be responsible for the commercial finance and business partnering activities for the commercial division of the Group. You will drive capital allocation decisions through strategic business planning, budgeting, and regular reviews with the business. You will be a key financial business partner to the Directors and Senior Managers within the leadership Team. This team works collaboratively with other finance functions including the Shared Services Centre to ensure that finance processes and ways of working are adhered to and operating effectively. You will be responsible for performance monitoring, reviewing business cases and requests for capital investment, supporting the business in obtaining budget for purchasing and initiating efficiency programs to improve cost intensity. The role requires someone who is highly organised, has strong attention to detail, is naturally inquisitive and has a proactive approach to improving processes and developing new ways of reporting. **Duties:** * Work closely with, and influence, senior management to help drive business performance –helping to ensure high quality financial decisions are made * Provide objective financial advice and opinions that positively impact the future choices of the business * Identify and drive financial efficiency with Commercial leadership team \- ensuring that resources are allocated effectively with rigorous pressure on costs and a commitment to a value\-for\-money approach * Monthly performance reviews with the CFO * Detailed cost benchmarking and propose measures to increase cost efficiency across the Group * Smooth annual planning processes for both multiyear strategic plans, financial budgets and monthly forecasts * Responsible for leading the Commercial Monthly Cost FP\&A processes, encompassing KPIs, Profit and Loss, on a timely and accurate basis for Senior Leadership and CFO review * Annual Budget process and preparation of materials for CEO sign\-off. **Requirements:** * Recently Qualified ACA (ideally Big 4 trained) with up to 1 year PQE * Financial planning experience advantageous, but not essential * Strong systems skills (Excel, PowerPoint, databases (Hyperion – Planning \& HFM), Oracle, etc.) * Experience in previous business partner role advantageous, but not essential * Financial modelling skills * Ability to analyse trends with a view to understanding key drivers and how they impact those trends both historically and in the future. * Ability to translate complex finance analysis into layman terms for communication to business. * The ability to communicate effectively and efficiently with the business while ascertaining the key impact/detail behind the issue/request. * Ability to work in a fast paced environment and embrace change. ***Interested applicants should email their CV to sarah.kelly@afrecruitment.ie***

title: Commercial Internship Program: Explore Marketing, Sales, Customer Success and Business Operations
description: MediaHQ is a market\-leading all\-in\-one media contacts database and press release distribution tool. Our platform is designed for PR and Communications teams to find media contacts, send press releases, analyse results and get more coverage. We are a dynamic and growing company and are looking for a Commercial Intern to join our Commercial Team. This is a great opportunity for someone who is looking to develop their career in Marketing, Sales, Customer Success or Business Operations and gain experience in a fast\-paced and innovative environment. This is a 12 month internship that can be done as part of your placement requirements for university or as a recent graduate. In 2009 the company started its cloud\-based software product and it has quickly grown into the largest media intelligence and press release sharing platform in Ireland. The service is now used by hundreds of customers including the Department of Health, Paddy Power and Fáilte Ireland. MediaHQ’s customer led philosophy is grounded in the notion that our products must always strive to make our customers feel better about themselves. We try to identify those emotions in every point of contact. The successful candidate will work in our dynamic commercial internship programme, they will have the opportunity to explore Marketing, Sales, Customer Success and Business Operations. This hybrid role means hands\-on involvement in the commercial and operations departments providing practical experience in all functions. There is a current requirement to come to the office (based in Harold’s cross, Dublin 6W) a minimum of one day a week. You'll work closely with the senior team, gaining valuable skills in customer support, marketing execution and sales enablement, with strong growth potential. **Do You Have** * Strong written and verbal communications skills * An approach based on self\-motivation, drive, intent and ideas. * Experience balancing multiple priorities through work, study, societies or projects. * Excellent knowledge and a regular user of social media including X and LinkedIn. * Strong teamwork skills. The focus of the commercial internship role will involve a well\-rounded engagement in Marketing, Sales, Customer Success and Business Operations functions. **Marketing** The role will focus on and include: * Assist in the creation and execution of marketing campaigns. * Contribute to content creation for various marketing channels. * Participate in marketing research and analysis to identify trends and opportunities. **Sales** * Support the Sales Team in prospecting and lead generation. * Assist in creating a database of leads, ensuring accurate and up\-to\-date information. * Qualify leads and pass along the sales pipeline. **Customer Success** * Engage with customers to understand their needs and concerns. * Contribute to customer onboarding and training processes. * Collaborate with the team to address customer feedback and enhance user satisfaction. **Business Operations** * Assist in the management and reporting of company financials. * Contribute to event management across the company. * Collaborate with the team to improve, streamline and ensure legal compliance for all internal processes and policies. **Cross Functional Collaboration** * Work collaboratively with team members from different functions. * Gain exposure to how various departments within the commercial section contribute to overall business success. **Hands\-On Experience** * Actively participate in day\-to\-day tasks and projects within the commercial section. * Learn and apply practical skills related to marketing, sales, customer success and business operations. * Rotate through different functions to gain a comprehensive understanding of the business. **Analytical Skills Development** * Analyse data related to marketing effectiveness, sales performance and customer satisfaction. * Provide insights and recommendations based on data analysis. At MediaHQ, we practise growth mindset principles—we see possibility instead of limitation. If you’re curious, want to learn how a business works and enjoy working in a fast\-moving but supportive environment, we’d love to hear from you. **Application** Please apply with your CV (no more than two pages). We also require a short cover letter (max one page). Please, no stock cover letters.

title: Commercial Revenue Analyst- FMCG
description: Amicus Recruitment has an excellent opportunity for a **Commercial Analyst** with a leading Global FMCG Multinational .So why is this an excellent opportunity ? It is their brands ; calibre of people in place\- and the learnings you will obtain With offices worldwide and with an iconic brand portfolio; you cannot get any bigger or better! Importantly however, there is a genuine and collaborative culture with some of the best people within the FMCG sector in situ and if successful, there is room for career progression. I had a really good meerting with Karen \- the Head of Commercial Operations\- ( Karen is a Diamond of a person\- experienced; sharp yet very personable ) and she said " *we can be a complex business in that there can be red\- tape and processes to follow; but on the plus side, a number of our brands are number 1\- our customers need our brands to succeed and we have the best in class activations and as you know Denis people consistently move in to different areas and business units and you / the incoming Analyst will obtain fantastic learnings. I have been here close to 6 years and I love working here"* **\*\* To be considered for this role, you must have at least 3 years FMCG experience in a buyer / commercial or analytical role \- and reside in Ireland \*\*** **Key Duties** * Own Customer \& Channel input to the S\&OP process driving forecast accuracy. * Design for channel, retail and pricing \& promo mix optimisation, sku simplification, and Trade Terms optimisation for Fuel for Growth. * Partner with Supply Chain team in the Demand Review Meeting gathering key demand driving inputs from customer and brand teams to ensure the Unit has the appropriate demand forecast to support in\-market activity. * Track and drive total category/channel business performance. * Monitor category/brand/channel market share, financial and execution performance to address issues and opportunities. Lead gap closing initiatives \& interventions. * Responsible for SKU reduction process and Business waste (Residuals). * Own resolutions on trading issues that affect more than one customer (eg promo strategy / volume allocations / 1st to market NPD launches) * Work closely with Customer \& Finance teams on optimising promotional sell\-out (trade investment, volume delivery \& profitability) **KEY SKILLS \& KNOWLEDGE** * Results driven/achievement focused, strong focus on business outcomes, gap closure etc. Makes decisions in ambiguous circumstances * Entrepreneurship and Experimentation: Leads/takes action as if it was own business. Leverages digital technologies to simplify work and improve communication. * Organizational agility: Develops and leverages strong internal and external networks effective at stakeholder management \& strategic influencing. * Strong understanding of the Ireland retailer landscape and how it’s evolving * Digital connected world – understands the path to purchase and embeds connected world framework into strategies and priorities. For more information, please contact Denis Mac Sweeney on 087 977 2742 for a confidential call or email Denis at denis@amicus.ie if you wish to apply for the role.

title: Competition Associate (Solicitor) - Top Tier Firm
description: Associate – Competition \& Regulated Markets (2–4 PQE) \| Dublin A leading law firm in Dublin is currently seeking to recruit an Associate (2–4 years PQE) to join its Competition \& Regulated Markets team. This is an excellent opportunity for a lawyer with experience in Irish and/or EU competition law to join a highly regarded team advising domestic and international clients on a broad range of competition and regulatory matters. **The Role Will Involve Advising On Areas Such As** * Competition law aspects of mergers and acquisitions, including merger control filings * Cartel and abuse of dominance investigations * Competition compliance and advisory work * Regulatory matters across a range of sectors **The Ideal Candidate Will Have** * 2–4 years’ post\-qualification experience in competition law * Experience advising on Irish and/or EU competition matters * Strong academic credentials and excellent analytical skills * The ability to work as part of a collaborative and high\-performing team This role offers the opportunity to work on high\-profile, complex matters, develop your expertise in competition and regulatory law, and progress your career within a supportive and dynamic environment. If you would be interested in learning more about this opportunity, please feel free to contact me directly for a confidential discussion. Robert Walters Operations Limited is an employment business and employment agency and welcomes applications from all candidates

title: Corporate Services Administrator
description: **The Role** WPS is currently seeking a Corporate Services Administrator to join our dynamic and growing team in Dublin. In this role, you will work with a senior manager to oversee the compliance and regulatory obligations of our transactions and assist with the delivery of internal projects. This is an excellent opportunity to play a key role in a highly regarded and expanding organisation which places huge emphasis on continuous professional development and career progression within a collegiate team\-based environment. **Duties \& Responsibilities** You will have a broad range of responsibilities including: * Assist with the management of the quarterly board meeting cycle for a large number of SPV's; * Monitor regulatory and compliance deliverables and assist with ensuring these deliverables are met; * Prepare reports and presentations to assist the management team; * Assist with the delivery of internal projects and initiatives; * Ensure that key transaction data is recorded and kept up\-to\-date on internal records; * Oversee the administration of various data bases including internal data bases and external banking platforms; * Assist with various company secretarial tasks including company incorporations, annual compliance, filings including annual returns with the Companies Registration Office and other ad hoc duties; * Arrange board meeting logistics including agreeing board calendars, sending calendar invitations and diarising agreed dates with Board of Directors; * Assist the senior management with various administrative tasks, including arranging events, client meetings etc.; * Assist the team with managing disbursements and invoicing clients; * Demonstrates awareness and understanding of legal or regulatory developments and proactively seeks to ensure that the team is positioned to address those challenges; * compliance with internal policies and procedures, while actively contributing to process improvements within the team **Knowledge \& Experience** * A minimum of three years' experience in a relevant legal, financial services or corporate administration role is essential; * A university law or business graduate, with an excellent academic background; * A desire to work in a client facing environment where delivery of a professional service to both clients and other counterparties is of key importance; * Strong communication skills, an ability to think on their feet, excellent attention to detail and exceptional organisational skills are essential for this multi\-client role; * Completion of or currently studying for the company secretarial qualification (Grad ICSA) would be an advantage; * Experience with Viewpoint, Power BI and Oracle would be useful, but it is not essential. * Conscientious, organised and a team player who can work on their own and as part of a wider team; and * Enthusiastic in nature, with a real interest in learning and developing their skills. **Benefits** We offer excellent progression opportunities along with a competitive remuneration package and benefits including: * Discretionary annual bonus scheme * 23 days annual leave * Hybrid Working Policy – 3 days in the office * Health insurance cover * Pension scheme * Life Assurance * Income Protection * Taxsaver and Bike to Work schemes * Professional Membership Fees * Social Committee * Employee Assistance Programme * Wellness programme

title: Credit Analyst
description: We're currently supporting the recruitment for a position within a **Retail Valuations Centre of Specialisation** , based in either **Cork or Dublin.** The role offers a pay rate of **€18\.19–€20\.79** per hour, operates on a **hybrid model** with three days onsite, and is initially a **6‑month contract** with potential to extend. There are two potential team pathways depending on your experience: 1\. \*\*Operations Team\*\* – focused on managing the full Internal Monitoring Test (IMT) operational cycle, maintaining key valuation tools and databases, supporting governance requirements, and ensuring smooth workflow coordination. This suits someone who is detail‑oriented, organised, comfortable with high‑volume processes, and experienced in operations, compliance, risk, or data management. 2\. \*\*Valuations Team\*\* – responsible for preparing and reviewing valuation reports, applying valuation methodologies aligned to RICS/EVS standards, and collaborating with internal and external stakeholders. This is ideal for someone with a background in property valuation, credit, commercial real estate, or financial analysis. Both roles play a key part in ensuring robust, consistent, and well‑governed valuations that support wider lending decisions. If this aligns with your experience, could you please share an updated CV? I would also appreciate a quick 10‑minute call to discuss the role in more detail. Please let me know a suitable time. Looking forward to hearing from you.

title: Customer Contact Agent
description: **Who We Are** **At Cornmarket** , we look after the financial wellbeing of public servants and their families. We’ve been focused on public sector employees for over 50 years, learning how to bring our clients the best financial services – from car, home, health and life insurance, to income protection, retirement planning and beyond. **Role Purpose** We are looking for a Customer Contact Agent to join our Life Sales team. Customer Contact Agents are responsible for making appointments with existing clients, on behalf of our team of Financial Consultants, on a primarily outbound call basis. The role holder will be effectively managing the diaries of our Consultants to ensure that all appointments can go ahead as scheduled in line with our clients’ expectations. This is an entry\-level position within the Life Department, one which provides an ideal opportunity to individuals looking to carve out a future in financial services. The role holder will receive on\-the\-job training and coaching to enable them to excel in terms of their own contribution, with strong encouragement and reward to undertake QFA exams. **Accountabilities Of The Role** * Making outbound calls and taking calls from members of the public sector unions to arrange appointments with the company’s Consultants * Meeting monthly targets which are built around call volume and appointment scheduling (alongside these targets comes an excellent monthly bonus structure) * Dealing with clients in a professional and timely manner with a customer first approach * Cross\-selling other Cornmarket products and services * Performing tasks in a compliant manner to set procedures and prescribed scripts * Completing various administration duties, such as data entry, logging and scanning * Participating in weekly team meetings * Continuously looking for and suggesting ways to improve existing processes **What We Can Offer You** * Flexible working from home options as part of our Cornmarket Flex ways of working * Significant investments in your professional development (e.g. insurance qualifications, professional qualifications relevant to your role) * Annual leave (increases based on service) * Flexi leave (option to take additional annual leave) * Attractive flexible pension contribution rates * Health insurance or wellness subsidy * Fully paid family leave types (maternity/paternity/surrogacy) * Performance based bonus/commission * Income protection * Life cover * Discounts on financial products * Comprehensive health wellbeing programme * Employee Assistance Programme * Active Sports Social Club **Skills Knowledge Required** * Previous experience in dealing with customers over the phone or in a customer service role desired * Excellent verbal communication skills * Proven track record in meeting and exceeding targets * Strong IT Skills with good working knowledge of the MS office suite * Ability to adapt to and learn new tasks * Highly motivated to work to defined targets **Behavioural Competencies Required** * Drive for results and target driven * Approachable and a can\-do attitude * Excellent communication skills * Quality\-focused, Customer First approach * Strong attention to detail * Excellent PC Microsoft Word Skills * Ability to learn and carry out tasks in line with procedure * Team player ﻿ **Equal Opportunities Data Privacy Notice** Cornmarket is committed to building an inclusive workplace environment, We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer, striving to create a welcoming environment. All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to age, disability, ethnic background, family status, gender identify or expression, marital status, membership of the Traveller Community, national origin, race, religion, sexual orientation. We are also committed to providing reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with disabilities in the employment application process. To request an accommodation, please contact our Talent Acquisition Team. To review our data use practices, visit our Recruitment Data Privacy Notice.

title: Customer Success Associate - Dublin
description: As a Customer Success Associate, you will focus on the long term strategic relationship with our customers, drive adoption, and identify growth opportunities across a high volume of Datadog customers to ensure successful product use and retention. You'll be an advocate for the customer internally and focus on providing a positive experience for a high volume of accounts. Associates follow a well\-defined methodology to identify the customer's unique needs and clearly convey the value of the Datadog product. As we scale our Customer Success team, furthering personal development and team success is a top priority. *At Datadog, we place value in our office culture \- the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work\-life harmony that best fits them* **What You'll Do:** * Identify target opportunities, manage the full sales cycle and proactively build strategic relationships with customers and focus on retention. * Manage requests from your book of business such as but not limited to technical support escalations, billing questions, documentation/enablement requests to ensure a positive customer experience * Pivot account management strategy based on different customer requirements and needs seamlessly and efficiently * Act as an advisor to customers to ensure they're leveraging the solution effectively * Monitor and identify usage trends to uncover renewal risks and support greater adoption rates * Collaborate cross\-functionally with internal Datadog teams (support, product, finance, and legal) **Who You Are:** * Customer\-minded, always trying to provide the most value possible * Driven and motivated by a career in sales with a strong desire to grow a career in Customer Success. * A strong communicator (written and verbal) with excellent attention to detail * Experienced in independently negotiating contract terms and overcoming pricing objections for up\-sell/cross sell activities * 0\-2 years of experience in a customer account facing role and desire to grow a career in Customer Success * Passionate about building long term and lasting relationships *Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you're passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.* **Benefits and Growth:** * Best\-in\-breed onboarding * Sales training in MEDDIC and Command of the Message * An inclusive company culture, opportunity to join our Community Guilds * Intra\-departmental mentor and buddy program for in\-house networking * Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing * New hire stock equity (RSU) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP) * Generous global benefits *Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.* **About Datadog:** Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers' entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people\-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. Learn more about \#DatadogLife on Instagram, LinkedIn, and Datadog Learning Center. **Equal Opportunity at Datadog:** Datadog is proud to offer equal employment opportunity to everyone regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, and other characteristics protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Here are our Candidate Legal Notices for your reference. Datadog endeavors to make our Careers Page accessible to all users. If you would like to contact us regarding the accessibility of our website or need assistance completing the application process, please complete this form. This form is for accommodation requests only and cannot be used to inquire about the status of applications. **Privacy and AI Guidelines:** Any information you submit to Datadog as part of your application will be processed in accordance with Datadog's Applicant and Candidate Privacy Notice. For information on our AI policy, please visit Interviewing at Datadog AI Guidelines.

title: Data Analyst
description: As a Data Analyst, you will play a critical role in strengthening data governance, analytics, and oversight across Barclays Europe. You will partner with business, risk, and technology stakeholders to deliver high\-quality data insight, support regulatory and control requirements, and enable strategic decision\-making. To be successful as a Data Analyst, you will have: * Extensive experience in data analysis, data governance, and data management within financial services. * The ability to work within risk, control, or regulatory\-driven environments. * An excellent understanding of data quality, controls, lineage, and metadata concepts. * A background of senior stakeholder management, coupled with excellent communication skills. You may be assessed on the key critical skills relevant for success in role, such as risk and controls, change and transformation, business acumen strategic thinking and digital and technology, as well as job\-specific technical skills. This role will be located at our Dublin office. **Purpose of the role** To enable data\-driven strategic and operational decision making through extracting actionable insights from large datasets, performing statistical and advanced analytics to uncover trends and patterns, and presenting findings through clear visualisations and reports. **Accountabilities** * Investigation and analysis of data issues related to quality, lineage, controls, and authoritative source identification, documenting data sources, methodologies, and quality findings with recommendations for improvement. * Designing and building data pipelines to automate data movement and processing. * Apply advanced analytical techniques to large datasets to uncover trends and correlations, develop validated logical data models, and translate insights into actionable business recommendations that drive operational and process improvements, leveraging machine learning/AI. * Through data\-driven analysis, translate analytical findings into actionable business recommendations, identifying opportunities for operational and process improvements. * Design and create interactive dashboards and visual reports using applicable tools and automate reporting processes for regular and ad\-hoc stakeholder needs. **Assistant Vice President Expectations** * To advise and influence decision making, contribute to policy development and take responsibility for operational effectiveness. Collaborate closely with other functions/ business divisions. * Lead a team performing complex tasks, using well developed professional knowledge and skills to deliver on work that impacts the whole business function. Set objectives and coach employees in pursuit of those objectives, appraisal of performance relative to objectives and determination of reward outcomes * If the position has leadership responsibilities, People Leaders are expected to demonstrate a clear set of leadership behaviours to create an environment for colleagues to thrive and deliver to a consistently excellent standard. The four LEAD behaviours are: L – Listen and be authentic, E – Energise and inspire, A – Align across the enterprise, D – Develop others. * OR for an individual contributor, they will lead collaborative assignments and guide team members through structured assignments, identify the need for the inclusion of other areas of specialisation to complete assignments. They will identify new directions for assignments and/ or projects, identifying a combination of cross functional methodologies or practices to meet required outcomes. * Consult on complex issues; providing advice to People Leaders to support the resolution of escalated issues. * Identify ways to mitigate risk and developing new policies/procedures in support of the control and governance agenda. * Take ownership for managing risk and strengthening controls in relation to the work done. * Perform work that is closely related to that of other areas, which requires understanding of how areas coordinate and contribute to the achievement of the objectives of the organisation sub\-function. * Collaborate with other areas of work, for business aligned support areas to keep up to speed with business activity and the business strategy. * Engage in complex analysis of data from multiple sources of information, internal and external sources such as procedures and practises (in other areas, teams, companies, etc).to solve problems creatively and effectively. * Communicate complex information. 'Complex' information could include sensitive information or information that is difficult to communicate because of its content or its audience. * Influence or convince stakeholders to achieve outcomes. All colleagues will be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Values of Respect, Integrity, Service, Excellence and Stewardship – our moral compass, helping us do what we believe is right. They will also be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Mindset – to Empower, Challenge and Drive – the operating manual for how we behave.

title: Data Analyst (Financial),
description: Optum is a global organization that delivers care, aided by technology to help millions of people live healthier lives. The work you do with our team will directly improve health outcomes by connecting people with the care, pharmacy benefits, data and resources they need to feel their best. Here, you will find a culture guided by diversity and inclusion, talented peers, comprehensive benefits and career development opportunities. Come make an impact on the communities we serve as you help us advance health equity on a global scale. Join us to start **Caring. Connecting. Growing together.** Serving virtually every dimension of the health system, we work with a diverse set of clients across 150 countries \- from those who diagnose and treat patients to those who pay for care, deliver health services, and those who supply the cures. Optum maintains operations across North America, South America, Europe, Asia Pacific and the Middle East. Our innovative partnerships provide technology and tools that enable unprecedented collaboration and efficiency. As a result, we can tap into valuable health care data to uncover insights and develop strategies for better care at lower costs. **About The Role** As our **Data Analyst** you will join an established team within OptumRx responsible for managing our network pharmacy pricing guarantees. You will provide financial modelling and analytics related to the performance of effective rate pricing guarantees, specific to the Commercial business. You will work with internal stakeholders to drive to best outcomes and ensure alignment with our pharmacy contractual obligations. Within the role, you will build relationships with our partners and stakeholders across many functions, including Network Contracting, Client Contract Management, Finance, Legal and more. We're looking for someone with a quantitative mindset, attention to detail, a solid aptitude for complex problem solving, industry experience, and well\-developed communication and technical skills. Careers with Optum offer flexible work arrangements and individuals who live and work in the Republic of Ireland will have the opportunity to split their monthly work hours between our Dublin or Letterkenny office and telecommuting from a home\-based office in a hybrid work model. **Primary Responsibilities** * Support the monitoring and reporting of pricing guarantees for network pharmacies within Optum's Commercial book of business * Produce and maintain financial models and projections, ensuring inputs, assumptions, and calculations are accurate, well documented, and reviewed regularly * Collaborate with internal partners to gather required inputs and ensure models reflect contractual and operational considerations, maintaining a solid focus on precision and consistency in results * Identify data issues, emerging trends, or potential risks and escalate appropriately with clear supporting analysis * Communicate findings clearly through written summaries, reports, and presentations tailored to internal stakeholders You will be rewarded and recognized for your performance in an environment that will challenge you and give you clear direction on what it takes to succeed in your role, as well as providing development for other roles you may be interested in. **Required Qualifications** * Proven solid experience with SQL and/or Python, with the ability to perform effective and efficient querying involving multiple tables and subqueries * Demonstrated communication skills, able to work with stakeholders from multiple levels and disciplines **Preferred Qualifications** * Proven hands\-on experience with MS Azure * Proven PBM or Pharma experience * Proven experience in financial modelling * Proven ability to be comfortable analyzing and interpreting large volumes of data * Demonstrated ability to understand complex scenarios and quickly provide effective decisions **Please note you must currently be eligible to work and remain indefinitely without any restrictions in the country to which you are making an application. Proof will be required to support your application.** *At UnitedHealth Group, our mission is to help people live healthier lives and make the health system work better for everyone. We believe everyone\-of every race, gender, sexuality, age, location and income\-deserves the opportunity to live their healthiest life. Today, however, there are still far too many barriers to good health which are disproportionately experienced by people of color, historically marginalized groups and those with lower incomes. We are committed to mitigating our impact on the environment and enabling and delivering equitable care that addresses health disparities and improves health outcomes \- an enterprise priority reflected in our mission.* *Diversity creates a healthier atmosphere: Optum is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, disability, religion, age, race, and membership of the Traveller community, or any other characteristic protected by law. Optum is a drug\-free workplace. © 2026 Optum Services (Ireland) Limited. All rights reserved.* \#BBMEMEA

title: Data Review Analyst
description: As a Data Review Analyst, you will play a key role in supporting the Match Group Shared Care \& Moderation Services Team by completing a variety of labeling requests. This role involves both straightforward labeling tasks and more nuanced analysis across multiple platform ecosystems. You’ll apply your understanding of Trust \& Safety, detection systems, and moderation workflows to ensure consistent, accurate, and high\-quality review and annotation. You’ll be part of a cross\-functional, collaborative team that directly supports product, data science, analytics, and policy teams through scalable, insight\-driven data labeling and review. **How you’ll make an impact:** - Complete task requests, including: - Structured labeling and annotation tasks - Ad hoc data reviews requiring critical thinking and contextual judgment - Analyze patterns and surface insights across labeling outputs - Follow documented guidelines to ensure consistency and accuracy in decision\-making - Maintain confidentiality and data integrity across sensitive datasets **We may be a Match if:** - Prior experience in Trust \& Safety, content moderation, QA, or data labeling - Strong attention to detail and a commitment to high\-quality output - Clear written and verbal communication, especially when documenting rationale or edge cases - Ability to interpret platform policies and apply them consistently across data review tasks - Comfort working independently in a structured queue\-based environment - Experience working across multiple brands, languages, or international markets - Willingness to learn and adapt across different platform ecosystems - Comfort with ambiguity in early\-stage projects or exploratory analysis **Why Match Group?** Our mission is simple – to help people find love and happiness! We love our employees too and understand the importance of all life's milestones. Here are some of the benefits we are proud to offer: **Mind \& Body** – Medical, mental health, and wellness benefits to support your overall health and well\-being **Financial Wellness** – Competitive compensation, 100% employer match on 401k contributions up to 10% (cap at $10,000\), as well as an employee stock purchase program to help you feel supported in your financial security **Unplug** – Generous PTO and 14 paid holidays so you can unplug **Career** – Annual training allowance for professional development and ERG membership opportunities and events so you feel connected and empowered in your work **Family** – Families come in all shapes and sizes so we offer 20 weeks of 100% paid parental leave, fertility, adoption, and child care resources, as well as pet insurance and discounts **Company Gatherings** – We host company events where our employees get to know each other and build a sense of connection and belonging! We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and we value the rich dynamics that diversity brings to our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, creed, national origin, ancestry, disability, marital status, age, sexual orientation, sex (including pregnancy and sexual harassment), gender identity or expression, uniformed service or veteran status, genetic information, or any other legally protected characteristic. Period. *If you require a reasonable accommodation to participate in the hiring process — such as during pre\-employment testing or interviews — please indicate this by selecting “Yes” in the accommodation request field. We’ll reach out to discuss your needs if you're selected for the interview stage.* \#MG .

title: Digital Engineering and BIM Roles
description: At Mace, our purpose is to redefine the boundaries of ambition. We believe in creating places that are responsible, bringing transformative impact to our people, communities and societies across the globe. To learn more about our purpose, culture, and priorities, visit our strategy site. Within our consult business we harness our unique combination of leading\-edge practical expertise and project delivery to unlock the potential in every project. **The project:** The project is a generational project providing Dublin and Ireland with a much\-needed world class Metro system to support the fast\-growing population. The project is an 18\.8km fully autonomous Metro system, consisting of 16 stations, 2 significant tunnelled section and a number of significant structures along the alignment. The project will integrate existing transportation systems through the city and surrounding areas, including the Dublin airport, Irish Rail and the LUAS. The successful candidate must lead by example and serve as a role model ensuring the highest standards are adopted across the project. Our values shape the way we consult, and define the people we want to join us on our journey: **Safety First \- Going Home Safe And Well** * You will be an advocate of Mace's value of safety first, and be accountable for leading and maintaining exceptional safety, quality, cost, programme, sustainability and project compliance standards. * You will possess technical expertise in local health and safety rules and regulations relevant to project portfolio. * You will champion a diverse and inclusive working environment and understand the importance of the wellbeing of the people you manage. **Client Focus – Deliver On Our Promise** * BIM is the keystone of modern delivery of high\-end infrastructure projects. It creates efficiencies and provides information that would otherwise cause substantial delays to the projects we work on in Mace. * BIM and digital engineering combined coupled with Delivery are here to drive forward major infrastructure projects across Rail, Water and Energy. We require a number of BIM individuals across a number of grades from Junior to Experienced Senior Candidates to work on Rail projects in Dublin. **You’ll Need To Have** * Autodesk desktop products. * Validation and verification tools. * Visualisation tools. * Databases. * Content management library systems. * Bentley product suite (ProjectWise, AssetWise, iTwin). * Understanding of ISO19650, BS1192, common data environment (CDE), IFC, COBie, and classification (Uniclass), international cost management standard (ICMS). * Rail/Metro experience (including traffic management, handover and phasing drawings). * 5\+ years' experience in delivering BIM projects according to international standards. **You’ll Also Have** * Certification in BIM management, experience with GIS. * Adobe products. Mace is an inclusive employer and welcomes interest from a diverse range of candidates. Even if you feel you do not fulfil all of the criteria, please apply as you may still be the best candidate for this role or another role within our organisation. We are also open to discussing part time, flexible, and hybrid working options if suitable within the role. Please remember the Employee Referral Scheme and if there are any talented people you can recommend, just click the button and use the ‘Refer a friend’ feature found within the relevant job. \-site

title: Digital SCM - Spark Foundry
description: #### **The Role** **OVERVIEW** This role assumes responsibility for managing the integration of digital aspects of the media planning activity and providing digital strategy planning insights for multiple client accounts within their client team. It requires management of processes, excellent attention to detail and time management skills to ensure best in class delivery on behalf of the media team for clients. This role supports the Digital Client Director in fostering strong relationships with clients. The role requires an ambitious, creative and innovative person who prides themselves on building strong and trustworthy client relationships. Digital Senior Client Managers require strong collaboration and communication with the wider team, particularly Social, Search and Programmatic teams in Investment. The ideal candidate will enjoy working in a fast\-paced environment with a drive to succeed and can multi\-task in a deadline driven atmosphere. **Responsibilities** This role will have the responsibility of developing the digital media rationale for their clients which, in turn, must be aligned to the overall strategic direction of the client. This role will also be the key day to day client contact and will ensure that all media decisions are made using a robust process powered by the appropriate media planning tools. Central to this role is a clear understanding of digital media planning principles, an ability to articulate the strengths and weaknesses of each media channel and the role that each will play in delivering on the overall strategy. This role will also establish the KPIs for the media plan and will ensure that all important information is clearly shown on the plan for client approval. The Digital Senior Client Manager will also manage the implementation and delivery of the digital media plan and will perform the required Quality Control to ensure that the plan produced matches the media rationale and the objectives set. Success will be defined at the yearend RADAR client service review in improvement in media planning and the impact of media ideas on the client’s business. **AGENCY PERFORMANCE** * Understanding of the key business challenge for their clients as well as the digital media strategy to help address these challenges * Responsibility for delivery of the digital media rationale and the digital media plan to align with the overall media strategy * Participate in planning sessions and work with the Digital Client Director to deliver an integrated media response to the client briefs * Input into Macro channel budget setting as well as establishing the media KPIs for briefing to Investment teams * Development of digital media brief and the coordination of internal briefings across Core Practices and Central Teams * Day to day client service and working closely with the Digital Client Manager to deliver to deadlines * Manage the quality control of the media plan to ensure that it matches the brief and the media rationale and that all relevant KPIs, target audiences and objectives are clearly outlined on the plans * Ensure any audit recommendations that have been agreed by Business Director and Client Director with auditor are implemented on all media plans, including working with Channel Planning and Investment teams to manage * Working with Digital Client Managers to ensure plans are audit ready and KPI document is up to date for each audit * Adopt and embrace an accuracy culture by having and supporting excellent attention to detail * Manage the intra\-campaign reporting and optimisation with the specialist teams * Work with the client to change plans / budget allocation where necessary * Liaise with client to obtain plan approval * Engage with media owners \& working with them to deliver partnerships in an efficient and collaborative manner * Responsible for digital activity at audit **COMMERCIAL PERFORMANCE** * Demonstrate a strong comprehension of the client’s business and their KPIs * Ensure all projects are delivered on time and on budget * Manage media budgets across all plans and Practice areas * Have a clear understanding of the scope of the client contracts including the key performance metrics * Ensure all billing is 100% accurate and on time for clients, and particularly on new specialised service projects * Work with the Digital Client Manager, Finance and Digital Client Director to monitor and manage aged debt, ensuring clients pay in a timely fashion * Ensure timesheets are updated weekly and that they accurately reflect the time spent on our clients business * Work with Digital Client Director to monitor profitability and revenue targets for your clients * Garner insight needed for the development of the media strategy **TALENT MANAGEMENT** * Commit to the internal Core Learning programme(s) and regularly attend training for your development * Collaborate closely with the Digital Client Director to ensure your personal development requirements are met * Ensure a strong comprehension and commitment to your career development plan in line with your 360 appraisal, quarterly catch up’s and goals * Assist in the management and development of junior team members while adhering to the performance management process in place * Uphold Core’s values in words and actions * Proactively build relationships within the wider company * Actively participate in knowledge sharing across the company and within your team * Contribute to a hardworking and fun team environment #### **Your profile** **EXPERIENCE** * Previous digital media experience in a similar role, with a proven track record in media planning and buying * Previous experience working with digital media tools * Knowledge of the broader digital disciplines * Excellent working knowledge of Microsoft Office, Excel and PowerPoint * Experience project managing large scale, complex media campaigns is essential * High level of business and financial acumen * Possess good presentation skills – assist in presenting to clients **Person Specification** * Be a confident communicator * Excellent interpersonal skills – ability to build strong relationships with clients * Display the ability to think creatively and innovatively * Very knowledgeable on all media – ideally an expert on at least one medium * Possess good presentation skills – assist in presenting insights toclients * Good listener \- ability to understand client’s objectives * Excellent attention to detail * Understands the importance of upskilling * Can think on their feet to make the right decision quickly * Highly organised individual that can manage multiple client projects and the overall delivery of the work * Understands and appreciates the benefit of processes * Be responsive to requests * Know when to escalate and involve other people #### **Why us?** * Flexible and hybrid working model, within the Republic of Ireland * 24/7 access to professional development through LinkedIn Learning along with career progression and mentoring * The opportunity to work across a huge range of clients within so many different categories from sport, retail, food and drink, to alcohol, insurance, banking and so much more * 21 days annual leave plus a day off on your birthday, company closure days at Christmas and early finish on bank holiday Fridays * A company culture that promotes work\-life balance * Family\-focused benefits including paid maternity and paternity leave as well as maternity phase back program, fertility treatment supports and paid pregnancy loss leave * Access to mental health and well\-being programs including paid health insurance for you and your dependents under 18 * Professional financial advice; employer contributory pension and life assurance * Make a difference by joining our D\&I, CSR, Green, Social or Great Place to Work committees; you’ll get extra days leave for your efforts. * And lots more … #### **Salary Range** 48,000\-56,000 DOE #### **About us** Core is Ireland's largest marketing communications company with over 400 people working across the key areas of marketing communications \- research, data science, strategy, planning, creative development and implementation. We are now a collective of nine practices, working towards a single purpose \- to expand the possibilities of what brands can achieve. At Core, we believe that change can move us forward and compel us towards new possibilities. Our wonderful people are at the centre of our success. It is essential that we keep building a high\-trust working environment that supports our people and enables them to flourish. There is no magic formula for becoming a great place to work. Instead, it involves working together, through constant change, to match the personal dreams and ambitions of our people with the aspirations of the organisation. Over the years, we have cemented our position as a great place to work, with 2025 being voted 8 Best Large Workplace in Ireland. Additionally, we are recognised as a Best Workplace for Women, and Best Workplace for Health \& Wellbeing. 2025 was our best year yet, and we're excited to have you join us and our continued success.

title: Educator - Part Time
description: Cairdeas Kinnegad Community Childcare, a welcoming and vibrant creche located in Kinnegad, is offering an **Internship Opportunity** for a recent graduate (QQI Level 8 in Childcare) to join our ECCE program (21 hours) and gain valuable hands\-on experience in a busy and dynamic early years setting. Our service is committed to providing high\-quality early years education in a safe, inclusive, and nurturing environment. This internship is designed to support newly qualified educators in developing their professional skills and confidence. This role also offers flexible extra hours for those interested in additional experience. **Requirements:** * QQI Level 8 qualification in Childcare (recent graduates encouraged to apply) * Strong knowledge of early childhood development * Understanding of play\-based emergent curriculum and the Aistear framework * Willingness to learn and develop professional practice * Ability to work as part of a team and show initiative * Warm, patient, and positive attitude with children * Availability to work during summer **What We Offer:** * Valuable hands\-on experience in a supportive environment * Mentoring and guidance from experienced educators * Opportunity to build professional skills and confidence * Potential for future career opportunities If you are a recent graduate passionate about early childhood education and would like to start your career in a friendly and supportive team in Kinnegad, please submit your CV and cover letter.

title: Employment Lawyer
description: We are collaborating with a highly reputable global law firm as they expand their Dublin office. A unique opportunity has arisen for an employment lawyer (2\+ PQE) to become part of their growing team. You will benefit from a collegial and stimulating work atmosphere, complete with flexible arrangements and attractive salary packages. **For more details, please reach out to Megan Shannon, or apply directly here, and we will connect with you! All enquiries and applications are completely confidential.**

title: Executive Operations Associate - President's Office
description: **Company Description** It all started in sunny San Diego, California in 2004 when a visionary engineer, Fred Luddy, saw the potential to transform how we work. Fast forward to today — ServiceNow stands as a global market leader, bringing innovative AI\-enhanced technology to over 8,100 customers, including 85% of the Fortune 500®. Our intelligent cloud\-based platform seamlessly connects people, systems, and processes to empower organizations to find smarter, faster, and better ways to work. But this is just the beginning of our journey. Join us as we pursue our purpose to make the world work better for everyone. **Job Description** **What you get to do in this role:** The EMEA President's Office runs on operational precision and strategic momentum. This role exists to protect both, every single day. You will work directly with the Chief of Staff, serve as the connective tissue between the executive office and cross\-functional teams, and progressively take on operational leadership as you build trust and demonstrate judgment. This is not a traditional support role. It is a high\-accountability, high\-visibility position for someone who is already an exceptional EA and is ready for more. **Executive support (approx. 25%)** * Own the Chief of Staff's calendar with sharp scrutiny, protecting strategic time and proactively resolving conflicts before they land * Manage travel, bookings, and expense reporting end\-to\-end with zero follow\-up needed * Coordinate closely with the EA to the EMEA President to ensure non\-negotiable meeting alignment * Act as a reliable, discreet first point of contact for operational and administrative matters **Business Operations \& Project Management (approx. 75%)** * Set up and maintain President's Office operating cadences: weekly syncs, leadership forums, cross\-functional checkpoints * Track actions, milestones, and blockers across multiple workstreams, escalating proactively before issues become problems * Own the end\-to\-end planning and execution of major EMEA events: QBRs, SKO, Knowledge, Rise Together, president\-led office visits * Build and maintain SharePoint pages, OneNote structures, trackers, and insight dashboards the team actually uses * Support the development of executive\-ready presentations and briefing materials * Reinforce connection points between central and regional teams, ensuring operational clarity across a matrixed organization **Qualifications** **To be successful in this role you have:** * 5\+ years as an Executive Assistant in a fast\-paced tech or SaaS environment, ideally supporting C\-suite or a President's Office * A track record of reaching beyond your title: you have led projects, built systems, or taken ownership of things nobody asked you to own * You have felt frustrated when your role was purely logistical, and you have done something about it * Highly organized with strong written and verbal English, and the confidence to communicate with senior stakeholders directly * Fluent in SharePoint, OneNote, Excel, PowerPoint, and Smartsheet or equivalent * Global mindset and genuine curiosity about how a large, matrixed organization operates **Additional Information** **Work Personas** We approach our distributed world of work with flexibility and trust. Work personas (flexible, remote, or required in office) are categories that are assigned to ServiceNow employees depending on the nature of their work and their assigned work location. Learn more here. To determine eligibility for a work persona, ServiceNow may confirm the distance between your primary residence and the closest ServiceNow office using a third\-party service. **Equal Opportunity Employer** ServiceNow is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, sexual orientation, national origin or nationality, ancestry, age, disability, gender identity or expression, marital status, veteran status, or any other category protected by law. In addition, all qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with legal requirements. **Accommodations** We strive to create an accessible and inclusive experience for all candidates. If you require a reasonable accommodation to complete any part of the application process, or are unable to use this online application and need an alternative method to apply, please contact globaltalentss@servicenow.com for assistance. **Export Control Regulations** For positions requiring access to controlled technology subject to export control regulations, including the U.S. Export Administration Regulations (EAR), ServiceNow may be required to obtain export control approval from government authorities for certain individuals. All employment is contingent upon ServiceNow obtaining any export license or other approval that may be required by relevant export control authorities. From Fortune. ©2025 Fortune Media IP Limited. All rights reserved. Used under license.

title: FP&A Analyst
description: A leading global investment manager is building out its Finance team and this role sits right at the centre of it. This is not a back\-office reporting role. It’s a commercial, forward\-looking FP\&A seat with real visibility to senior leadership and direct involvement in decision making. **The Role** * Own monthly financial dashboards for senior management * Analyse revenue, cost drivers and KPIs to improve forecasting accuracy * Partner with business leaders on budgeting and strategic initiatives * Build financial models to support product expansion and growth decisions * Work cross\-functionally with Risk, Strategy and HR on firm wide projects * Support financial reporting as required **The Opportunity** * Newly created team in Dublin \- genuine opportunity to shape and influence * Direct exposure to global FP\&A teams * Strong blend of commercial FP\&A \+ technical finance / reporting * High visibility role with interaction at senior stakeholder level * Fast\-paced, high\-performance environment where strong analysts thrive **Requirements** * 4\+ years FP\&A experience (financial services preferred) * Strong Excel (VBA / macros a plus) and analytical skillset * Ability to communicate clearly and influence stakeholders * Comfortable operating in a fast\-moving, high\-pressure environment

title: FULL TIME SALES ASSOCIATE 40H
description: Overview: **Company Overview** Founded in Paris in 1948, Longchamp is a world\-renowned luxury brand celebrated for its exceptional craftsmanship, fine leather goods, and modern Parisian design. From iconic handbags like the Le Pliage® to elegant accessories and ready\-to\-wear collections, Longchamp blends tradition with innovation. With boutiques in over 80 countries, the brand offers an elevated shopping experience grounded in French heritage and global appeal. As a key member of Longchamp’s boutique team, Sales Assistants play an essential role in creating a luxurious, welcoming, and personalised shopping experience. They are the frontline ambassadors of the brand, combining product knowledge, enthusiasm, and elegance to deliver exceptional service. **Values \& Culture** Longchamp embraces five core values: * Elegance – Providing a refined and polished customer experience * Authenticity – Acting with honesty and professionalism * Creativity – Encouraging innovation and fresh thinking * Quality – Striving for excellence in service and product * Sustainability – Committing to thoughtful, responsible business practices Sales Assistants embody these values through every client interaction and contribute to a culture of respect, collaboration, and passion for luxury retail. Responsibilities: **Job Purpose** To deliver exceptional, personalized service that exceeds client expectations, supports daily boutique operations, and contributes to achieving sales and Clienteling goals in alignment with Longchamp’s luxury standards. **Key Responsibilities** Customer Service \& Sales * Welcoming and advising customers: provide a warm, personalized welcome and guide clients through tailored recommendations based on their needs. * Contributing to sales: taking an active part in selling products in line with the company's sales techniques and store objectives. * Building long term relationship with clients: develop and maintain client relationships through excellent service and personalised follow\-up. * Managing returns and exchanges: dealing with customer requests for product returns or exchanges, in line with internal policies. **Product \& Brand Knowledge** * Demonstrate in\-depth knowledge of Longchamp products, craftsmanship, and brand heritage * Communicate brand stories and product features with confidence and clarity * Stay updated on new arrivals, seasonal launches, and promotional activities **Boutique Operations** * Ensuring product presentation: making sure that products are displayed in adherence to visual merchandising guidelines. * Restocking and stock management: support replenishment, stockroom activity, and inventory accuracy * Managing checkout operations: carrying out transactions smoothly and guaranteeing fast, secure service and data capture Qualifications: **Skills \& Qualifications** * Minimum 1 year of experience in retail, preferably luxury or premium * Strong interpersonal and communication skills * POS system knowledge and basic MS Office familiarity * Fluent in English (additional languages preferred) * Passion for fashion, customer service, and luxury brands **Person Specification** * Outgoing, polished, and professional demeanour * Service\-driven and problem solving * Team player with a proactive attitude * Detail\-oriented, punctual, and dependable * Organized, and able to prioritize daily tasks * Flexible with scheduling including weekends, evenings, and holidays

title: Fabric Sourcing Trainee
description: Because you can see how we can be better. Innovate, your way. Fabric Sourcing Trainee at Primark What’s a career at Primark all about? The positive impact you’re making, the experiences you’re having and the people you’re with. You’re our inspiration. Embrace what sets you apart, own your career and develop in ways you never expected. Leave your mark. And do it your way. **What You’ll Do as a** **Fabric Sourcing Trainee** We want you to feel challenged and inspired. Here, you’ll develop your skills across a range of responsibilities: * Work collaboratively with Design, Buying, Merchandising, and Quality teams to support seasonal development and prepare presentation materials. * Manage the sample library by logging, labelling, tracking sample progress, and following up with suppliers on outstanding items. * Communicate with global fabric mills and suppliers on sample requests, pricing, lead times, and technical details while supporting onboarding of new suppliers. * Maintain accurate data in sourcing systems, updating fabric charts, cost sheets, and ensuring all documentation is current and compliant. * Assist with fabric costing by gathering prices, preparing purchase requests and sample POs, and keeping sourcing trackers updated. * Research trends and product developments, preparing clear updates to support business needs and seasonal presentations. **What You’ll Ge****t**: People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: * 27 days of leave, pension. * Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. ***What You’ll Bring*** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * Strong organisational skills, excellent attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple tasks in a fast‑paced environment. * Clear and professional communicator (written and verbal) with strong interpersonal skills and the confidence to present and push back professionally. * Proficient in Microsoft Excel, Outlook, and office tools, with desirable experience in textiles, sourcing, product development, or PLM/ERP systems. * Proactive, solution‑focused, and resourceful, with a strong sense of ownership and accountability. * Able to build positive relationships, work collaboratively, and adapt to changing priorities and deadlines. * Operates with integrity, sound judgement, emotional intelligence, and a high level of professionalism and trust. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. ***About Primark*** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. Our fashion isn’t one\-size\-fits\-all and neither is our culture. Primark promotes equal employment opportunity, we strive to create an inclusive workplace where people can be themselves, access opportunities and thrive together. **REQ ID:** JR\-6970
