This is an excellent opportunity for someone with 1\+ year’s experience to step into a structured environment, gain strong site exposure and progress quickly under experienced senior engineers and site management. **Key Responsibilities** * Assisting with setting out for groundworks, foundations and housing units * Supporting the engineering team with site coordination and daily operations * Ensuring works are carried out in line with drawings, specifications and quality standards * Maintaining accurate site records and QA documentation * Liaising with subcontractors and ensuring work is completed to programme * Assisting with levels, surveys and site measurements * Supporting health \& safety compliance on site **Requirements** * Degree in Civil Engineering, Construction Management or related discipline * Minimum 1 year’s site experience (placement experience considered) * Strong understanding of setting out and site engineering basics * Good communication skills and ability to work as part of a team * Full driving licence **What’s on Offer** * Strong pipeline of residential projects in the Leinster region * Clear progression path into Site Engineer / Senior Engineer roles * Exposure to a well\-run, structured site environment * Supportive team with strong mentorship **Contact Stephen Kelly on 087 474 4071 to find out more!**

title: Junior Statistical Programmer
description: **Key Requirements** 1\. Third level degree in Statistics / Biomedical/Computer Science or related discipline. 2\. Experience in SAS or equivalent programming language (eg: R, SPSS). 3\. Excellent organisational, communication and time management skills. 4\. Excellent attention to detail. 5\. Ability to work autonomously as well as work in a team\-based environment 6\. Oncology background/experience an advantage. 7\. Proficiency in all Microsoft Office Tools and general computer literacy. **Main Function(s)** To perform Statistical Programming Tasks on assigned studies within Cancer Trials Ireland for the Biometrics unit. **Primary Responsibilities** * Work with Group Statistician in providing statistical support to Cancer Trials Ireland investigators regarding study design, endpoint selection and sample size calculation. * Provide statistical input to Case Report Form (CRF) and database design, also data management processes. * Create, validate, execute, and generate statistical programs consisting of listings, safety and efficacy outputs (analysis datasets, tables, figures) as planned in the Statistical Analysis Plan. * Writing, documenting, and performing quality control review of SAS programs * Program, test and document specific listings and reports (e.g. Safety Monitory Committee reports, Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) reports and End of Study Reports). * Develop tables, graphs for scientific publications as directed by Group Statistician. * Support Data Management activities including data review activity before database lock * Provide technical advice and programming solutions for clinical projects. * Creation and review of technical specifications and applicable Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) / Study\-specific procedures (SSP) / Work instructions. * Create and maintain study files and other appropriate study documentation. * Collaborate with team members to plan and deliver on Study Deliverables. * Adhere to all related procedures and regulations, e.g. Study Guidelines, SOPs, ICH GCP, etc. * Perform other tasks as specified by Head of Biometrics/Group Statistician.

title: Junior Supply Chain Consultant - September 2026
description: **Job Description** **About the role** **Kickstart your consulting career!** Get ready to launch your career in consulting, and join us! You’ll have a spot in our supply chain training boot camp at the beginning of September. You’ll sharpen your consulting expertise alongside fellow new starters from around the globe. Our training program covers everything from essential soft skills to advanced supply chain management to becoming a kick\-ass Supply Chain Consultant. Afterwards, you're ready to start on one of our running projects. **What can you do at Bluecrux?** * You'll start immediately in a visible, autonomous role as a project member in one of our running value chain projects in industries such as life sciences, chemicals, food and beverages, and manufacturing. * You'll see yourself working on and contributing to the main project phases. Here's an idea of what to expect: + Analyze and Optimize: You will always analyze, map, and conduct existing Supply Chain and Operations business processes. + Collaborate: Work closely with clients to understand their needs and make a real impact. Think about preparing and conducting project interviews to understand the client's needs better. You will become aware of the customer organization and be part of the change in their organization and ways of working. + Track and report: You'll track KPIs and report them accurately to the customer project team, ensuring clear, actionable insights. + Document and deliver: You create the most important customer and project deliverables, such as top\-notch project notes, guides and training material. * Here’s how you’ll continuously be making an impact: + Tell us, what’s your flavour? All of our projects share a common transformation mission, and once you join a project, you’ll be introduced to different extra layers of specialisation to add value. We will encourage you to explore and accelerate in Technology, Process knowledge, Data or Change Management. **Job Requirements** **About you** * You will graduate before September 2026 and want to start your career at the beginning of September 2026\. * You see yourself thriving in the world of value chain transformation and are interested in how Technology, Business Processes, Data Analytics, and Change management create a flourishing ecosystem of solutions. * You feel connected with and are ready to represent our company values: dig deep, own it, come together, move fast and be kind! * Above all, we believe in your potential: + You can demonstrate profound logical reasoning, and you are resourceful in detailing solutions and considerate about their consequences. + You're a team worker and player who can perform well without much supervision and who is committed to your own objectives. + You're open to feedback and always look for ways to enhance collaboration. + You like to be challenged daily, show proactive ownership and, above all, thrive in a fast\-paced environment. * Some hard skills: + Ideally, you have had an introduction to Supply Chain Processes in a minor or major within a master’s degree program, such as business engineering, civil engineering—operational research, applied economics, mechanical engineering or an MBA program. + You are fluent in English, the official work language in all of our offices. Mastery of any other language is a definite plus. + Willingness and eligibility to travel globally and to work in a hybrid working model. **Why join us?** * A kickstart to your career through our Supply Chain Bootcamp training program, at the beginning of September. You will learn the ins \& outs of Supply Chain Management: Demand Planning, S\&OP and Financial Planning, Supply Planning, Inventory Management, Master Data Management, Supply Chain Modelling and Planning Tools. This will allow you to onboard into our projects with a backpack full of expertise \& skills! * We know you by your first name, and don't let titles or levels withhold us from being truly aware of your talents. * You'll work on international projects and travel to client sites. From the start, you will gain exposure and recognition for every achievement at Bluecrux. * A Great Place To Work with lots of growth opportunities. We encourage initiative, so you’ll learn a lot quickly. Sometimes, you’ll fail fast, but we’ll help you to get up. We’ll enhance your personal and professional skills, and you can count on a great, supportive team of hands\-on, inspiring and enthusiastic supply chain colleagues. * Team events and fun include yearly Bluecrux weekends, last Friday meetings with drinks, team building, and sports activities. * Enjoy a flexible \& hybrid work environment, combining remote work, office presence and onsite client visits. * A competitive salary package, bonus program, net allowances, a smartphone with phone subscription and other benefits (such as a fridge full of drinks \& healthy snacks). **About us** At Bluecrux, we transform supply chains into smart, efficient value chains through a blend of expert consulting and cutting\-edge technology. We collaborate closely with industry leaders like Johnson \& Johnson, GSK, Bridgestone, AkzoNobel, Beiersdorf, Oatly, and many more – creating partnerships that drive meaningful impact. With a global presence in Europe, the US and APAC, we're committed to shaping the future of value chains, one success story at a time.

title: Landscape Operative - Training and Career progresson

title: Life Sciences Associate Consultant
description: Are you curious about science, medicine, and pharmaceuticals? Do you consider yourself to be a fast learner, proactive, and diligent? FENIX continues to experience significant growth and is seeking a Life Sciences Associate Consultant to support growing global consulting engagements. Our Life Sciences Associate Consultants leverage their analytic, interpretive, scientific, and communication skills to formulate well\-thought\-out insights that support team decision\-making and execution. FENIX Associate Consultants help derive customer insights through the conduct, management, and analysis of primary and secondary qualitative and quantitative research. They assist in writing concise, accurate summaries of industry and market events using their knowledge and context of the pharmaceutical industry as they contribute to successful project deliveries. In this role, you would have the opportunity to gain consulting experience from a team of FENIX experts, help guide and influence decision\-making for a global pharma company, and develop thought leadership in exciting therapy areas. **RESPONSIBILITIES:** * Conduct comprehensive secondary market research (e.g., monitoring competitor websites, earnings calls, clinical trials, and patient/HCP websites) * Identify and conduct primary market research (e.g., interviews of HCPs/KOLs, industry sources) and provide clear and accurate notes to the team * Assist junior team members with various market\-related tasks * Develop consistent and impactful primary CI * Cover key international conferences (either virtually or in\-person), including key clinical sessions, industry booths, and collect primary research * Develop a comprehensive congress planner * Interpret key clinical trials * Leverage problem\-solving skills and industry research methodologies to derive insights and inform project team and client decision\-making * Conduct quantitative research data analyses * ‪Leverage strong project management and organizational skills to ensure that all tasks are performed in a timely manner and that all client deliverable deadlines are met * Synthesize and communicate results to project teams and clients, leveraging consulting language * Assist in authoring relevant, clear, and succinct client implications, executive summaries, and elevator pitches * ‪Play an active role in deliverables, including client presentations and reports, as well as leveraging tools to strengthen client relations ‪ **QUALIFICATIONS:** * Excellent oral and written communication skills in English (fluent or Level C2 Certificate) **REQUIRED** * EU citizenship or the ability to legally live/work in Dublin, Ireland without the need for sponsorship **REQUIRED** * ‪Minimum of a master's degree in life sciences (e.g., biochemistry, pharmacology, biology), pharmaceutical business, or related field **REQUIRED** * Life sciences research experience/expertise **REQUIRED** * Good understanding of pharmacology (e.g., pharmacodynamics, routes of administration, receptor biology, signaling pathways) **REQUIRED** * 1\-2 years of experience in the pharmaceutical industry, life sciences research experience, and/or life sciences consulting **PREFERRED** * Attention to detail – Ability to meticulously review deliverables before submitting them for final QC (can you spot errors in your own work?) * Proactivity – at FENIX, we don't simply follow instructions; instead, we critically evaluate whether the instructions are complete or could be applied beyond their initial scope; in addition, providing new ideas on process improvements/efficiencies and methodologies is expected * Resourcefulness – the ability to independently search for an answer and ask for help when needed * Proficiency in the Microsoft Office Suite (Word, PowerPoint, Excel); the ability to craft visual and professional PowerPoint slides from scratch is a big plus * Demonstrated ability to work independently (good work ethic, maturity, and personal initiative), as well as ability to work as a member of a highly collaborative team * ‪Self\-discipline for planning and organizing tasks, ensuring that no task (no matter how minor) is forgotten * The ability to aggregate, organize, distill, and analyze relevant information to derive meaningful implications * Flexibility and ability to adapt to changing project and client needs (while you may initially begin on one project, you may have to do other projects based on changing company needs) * Willingness and ability to travel to domestic and international scientific congresses * Growth mindset, with the ability to work collaboratively as well as autonomously * Ability to think critically and strategically and make assessments keeping the "big picture" in mind, frequently asking yourself: "Does this make sense?" * Humility and ability to take feedback constructively, with a strong desire to learn and grow * The ability to take complex information and make it easy to understand to sometimes non\-scientific audiences

title: Maintenance Technician
description: Life changing therapies. Global impact. Bridge to thousands of biopharma companies and their patients. **We are PCI.** Our investment is in People who make an impact, drive progress and create a better tomorrow. Our strategy includes building teams across our global network to pioneer and shape the future of PCI. We are now looking for a **Maintenance Technician** (Permanent role) to join our team in Dundalk, County Louth. This is a **Day Shift** role, covering Monday to Wednesday 12 hours shift (7am to 7pm). **Summary Of Role** * Responsible for ensuring machine change over’s, stoppages and breakdowns are undertaken in a timely manner to targets and with minimum disruption to production schedules. * Undertake planned routine maintenance and maintain schedules ensuring that all equipment and plant are able to operate safely, efficiently, and in compliance with GMP practices. * Fault find to an expert level on Electrical and Mechanical systems. * Multiskilled able to program PLC’s and to participate in technically involved projects to improve or create new processes. * Mechanically able to design systems and develop to implementation. * Implement continuous improvements on equipment and carry out to a high standard within GMP guidelines. * Create MOC’s and manage through the QMS. * Create design of experiments and use other fault\-finding techniques to improve processes. **Responsibilities** * Ensure safe work practices are being followed at all times, report any near misses immediately to the EHS Department. * Ensure that maintenance areas and equipment are kept in a clean, tidy and secure state (including storage areas). * Adherence and recording of defined routine maintenance schedules; covering all equipment and plant. * Rapid and flexible attention to all breakdowns/stoppages to minimise loss of production, liaising with Engineering Supervisor and Operational Engineering Manager. * Carry out/Participate in A3 Problem solving activities on major issues and lead to Root cause. * Analysis of equipment redesign of any systems/equipment to improve process. * Planning and implementation of engineering improvement projects, designing and developing to Validation. * Advise Engineering Supervisor and Operational Engineering Manager of any machine or plant which requires major repair or maintenance. * Highlight potential areas for improvement within Production area regarding equipment or procedural changes. * Liaise with Production on identifying areas for continuous improvement and implement changes using correct change procedure. * Strip down, cleaning and reassembling of production equipment as per routine maintenance schedules. * Perform machine set\-ups and changeovers to target times as required in line with production requirements. * To actively assist in looking at ways to improve changeover times by procedure and method. * Ensure the timely and accurate completion of all documentation and paperwork such as tooling logs, setting sheets, online working sheets, PPM sheets. * Improve and maintain the levels of current Good Manufacturing Practice. * Assist with the training of colleagues as required. * The use of workshop equipment to manufacture piece parts and repair as required. * Assist with projects on trials, and new product introductions as required. * Carry out various other additional tasks that may be required from time to time in accordance with the overall goal of the company. **Essential** **Knowledge / Skills \& Experience:** * Advanced Certificate (Craft\- level 6 NFQ) in a mechanical or electrical discipline. Bachelor’s or Associate Degree or Diploma in Engineering / Certificate in Engineering. May substitute experience in lieu of educational requirements. * 4 years’ experience. * Proven track record of showing how results were delivered through management/leadership. * Capability to follow set clear goals, communicate these clearly and ensure progress to completion meeting predefined targets. * Ability to manage multiple tasks. * Problem Solving and Troubleshooting skills. The successful candidate should display a dynamic and proactive approach to manage problems. * Self\-motivated with a results driven approach. * Flexibility to work across different shifts on request in line with business needs. * Adaptable and ability to work collaboratively. * Proficient in Microsoft Office products (incl. Excel, PowerPoint, Word, etc.) * Take care of your own health and safety and that of people who may be affected by what you do (or do not do). * Co\-operate with others on health and safety, and not interfere with, or misuse, anything provided for your health, safety, or welfare. **Desirable** * Experience in a GMP or Regulated Environment. * Experience in Lean Manufacturing, including experience in Lean tools to develop continuous improvements. Join us and be part of building the bridge between life changing therapies and patients. Let’s talk future **Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) Statement** *PCI Pharma Services is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We do not unlawfully discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, age, sex, creed, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, marital or domestic or civil union status, familial status, affectional or sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetics, disability, military eligibility or veteran status, or any other protected status.* *At PCI, Equity and Inclusion are at the core of our company’s purpose: Together, delivering life\-changing therapies. We are committed to cultivating an inclusive workplace by holding ourselves accountable to the highest standards of understanding, fairness, respect, and equal opportunity – at every level. We envision a PCI community where everyone can belong and grow, and we strive to bring this vision to reality by continuously and intentionally assessing our people practices, policies and programs, marketing approach, and workplace culture.*

title: Managing Consultant
description: **MANAGING CONSULTANT** **Dublin** **Fixed Term Contract / Permanent** **Role Summary** This is a fantastic opportunity to join our fast\-growing Consulting Business working with some of the world’s leading financial institutions. As a **Managing Consultant**, the successful candidate will work directly with our clients to ensure they receive the quality consulting services that Delta Capita (DC) offers. The ideal candidate will have a deep understanding of financial services, exceptional problem\-solving skills, project management and business analysis experience, the ability to manage, motivate and inspire others, and a proven track record of delivering successful client engagements. You will have deep change management experience and prior success as a management consultant or within a change role in a financial services institution. To know more about our Consulting services, click here Consulting \- Delta Capita. **The Role and Responsibilities** This role is ideal for someone with the right mix of consulting and client management experience as well as having an entrepreneurial spirit. You will combine project delivery know\-how, a can\-do attitude, and a strong knowledge of Financial Services. **Project responsibilities include;** * Coordinate delivery of project tasks from initiation to closure, including definition of project scope and objectives, budgetary considerations, resource allocation \& training, formal documentation, delivery, monitoring and control, reporting and delivery assurance. * Day to day management and central point of contact for the project * Manage changes to the project scope, project schedule and project costs using appropriate verification techniques * Create and maintain all project documentation e.g., Terms of reference/ project charters, project plans, status reports. * Establish and maintain governance processes including stakeholder mapping, governance forums (e.g. Operating \& Steering Committees) and all supporting documentation * Design \& deliver documentation to support the successful operational implementation * Identify opportunities to streamline operational process relevant to the project * Manage and oversee project plan and RAID log (Risks, Assumptions, Issues, Dependencies) * Actively manage risks and issues, escalating to senior management and client stakeholders where required to ensure they are appropriately and quickly addressed, documenting as ‘lessons learnt’ once resolved * Build and manage relationships with project stakeholders, ensuring appropriate level and frequency of communication * Ensure decisions and outcomes are formally documented via minutes, sign\-offs, project documentation or any other client specific tools * Define, gather and collate meaningful KPIs and dashboards for management reporting * Undertake other Transformation \& Change lifecycle roles/responsibilities as required **As an experienced Managing Consultant, you will have:** * Proven Consulting experience or alternatively in Project Management or Business Analysis within Financial Services. * Change management experience: Strategy, Design, Analysis, Project Management, Build Testing, Go\-Live. * Understanding of Financial Services products and lifecycle events – Trade Finance experience is desired * Risk and control awareness in a banking environment * Experience managing projects and team members supporting successful project/programme deliveries. * Good knowledge of the capital markets and retail banking eco\-system and participants * You will come from a management consultant background or within a change role in a financial services institution. You must have a blend of both Financial Services knowledge and soft skills across influence, listening, presentation and team management. * As an experienced Consultant, you will be expected to be comfortable working on an independent basis and managing your own workload with minimal supervision. * You will also be responsible for helping to grow our business by supporting ongoing operations, RFPs and helping to manage junior consultants. **How We Work:** Delta Capita is an equal opportunity employer. We positively encourage applications from suitably qualified and eligible candidates regardless of age, colour, disability, national origin, ancestry, race, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, veteran status, genetic information, or any other status protected by applicable law. We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. If you require any reasonable adjustments through your interview process, please use the designated space within the application questionnaire. This is fixed term contract position located in Dublin, Ireland. As the selection and interview process is ongoing, please submit your application as soon as possible. If your profile is selected, a member of our team will contact you within 4 weeks. For this role, a valid working permit for Ireland is mandatory. **Who We Are:** Delta Capita Group (a member of the Prytek Group) is a global managed services, consulting, and solutions provider with a unique combination of experience in Financial Services and technology innovation capability. Our mission is to reinvent the financial services value chain providing technology based mutualized services for financial institutions for non\-differentiating services. **Our 3 offerings are:** * Managed Services * Consulting \& Solutions * Technology To know more about Delta Capita and our culture click here: Working at DC \- Delta Capita.

title: Manufacturing Process Technician
description: **Job Description Summary** The Manufacturing Process Technician at Amgen plays a crucial role in the operation and maintenance of manufacturing equipment. This individual is responsible for monitoring and adjusting manufacturing processes, troubleshooting any issues, and ensuring the quality of final products. They work in compliance with company policies and safety regulations. **Hiring Requirements:** * Manufacturing Process, Packing and Inspection Technicians are required to work a variety of shift patterns including days, 2, 3 and 4 cycle shifts. * They will perform all operations with due care and attention and in accordance with Good Manufacturing Practices and Amgen requirements. * They will be involved in problem solving and troubleshooting including initiation and documentation of investigations. * They will be responsible for the performance of self\-inspection Quality \& Safety Audits within their functional area. * They will be part of a learning and development program which will include annual reviews and goal setting via the Maximizing Amgen Performance (MAP) process. They will be responsible for the cross training of other colleagues. * They will be responsible for the use, review, revision and upgrade of operational documentation and peer review of operations. * They will play a key role in the development of the manufacturing systems and will be responsible for continuous improvement initiatives. Proactively identifying operational improvement opportunities or process related issues including escalation \& follow up with area Sr. Associate Tech Engineers for effective resolution and implementation. * They will be champions for safe working practices and safety initiatives within their functional area. * They will carry out any sampling, testing and inspections that may be required. They will also carry out calibration and maintenance of test equipment. * Contribute and assist with Corporate, FDA, IMB Auditors and other regulatory bodies during company audits. * They will carry out any other duty as may be designated by supervisor/ management from time to time. **Qualifications:** * Educated to Leaving Certificate standard or equivalent (minimum five passes including English and Mathematics required). * Applicants should have some relevant experience of operation of a manufacturing function in a sterile pharmaceutical manufacturing or similar environment. * Be a results\-oriented person with excellent organization, communication and team development skills with an ability to successfully interact with people, create a team environment and generate co\-operation. * Demonstrated ability to deliver to team, site and personal objectives. * Demonstrated understanding and use of RFT techniques and lean manufacturing concepts. **Preferred Skills:** Experience working with equipment utilised in the manufacture of parenteral products such as autoclaves, filling lines, formulation vessels plus: * Good mechanical aptitude * Excellent problem\-solving skills * Ability to work in a team environment and on one’s own initiative

title: Maria Tash, Sales Consultant, Full Time, Permanent
description: **GET TO KNOW US** MARIA TASH are the leading brand in fine jewellery and luxury piercing, with stores in New York, London, Dubai, Saudi Arabi, Kuwait, Dublin, Miami, LA and further plans for global expansion. **KNOW THE ROLE** We are looking for an exceptional Sales Associate to join our brand at our fun and fast\-paced concession. If you are a fashion forward individual with retail experience, preferably in the luxury sector, you could be just the person to join our team. **KNOW WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR** Ideally you will thrive in a fast\-paced environment, effortlessly work as part of a team, keep the shop floor immaculate whilst providing exceptional customer service. You must be sales driven and work cohesively as part of a team. We offer a great salary and benefits package, the chance for international travel, along with lots of opportunity for career development. **KNOW HOW WE WORK** **Experience is our business**: Our customer engagement sets the bar in service and experiential retail, giving our customers something to love every time. **We Are More Than Shops:** We create enjoyable places, to excite, where people socialise, discover, learn and are inspired, and where local communities are enriched. **We are Curators:** Our unique range of product and services are key to the sense of excitement that Brown Thomas Arnotts is renowned for, whether for a special day or every day, things that make our customer’s lives better. **We Care for Our People:** We believe a great customer experience starts with a great people experience. That is why we promote a culture where talent is nurtured and where creativity and learning are valued. **We Succeed by Working Sustainably:** We are community\-focused and our aim is to cultivate retail environments that are conscious, considerate and commercial.

title: Marketplace Advertising Graduate
description: **Join Luzern eCommerce:** Powering the World’s Most Ambitious Brands Step into the world of limitless possibilities with Luzern eCommerce, where we’ve been redefining online retail since 2003\. We partner with global icons like On Running, Wella, Nestlé, Mattel, Panasonic, HTC, and Philips to accelerate growth, maximize margins, and deliver exceptional customer experiences across Amazon, top marketplaces, branded webstores, and social platforms. Backed by significant investment, we’re on a mission to dominate the global eCommerce space. With our award\-winning team, cutting\-edge AI\-enabled Channel Optimizer platform, and bold strategies, we’re not just keeping up with the future of retail—we’re creating it. Be part of one of the fastest\-growing tech sectors, where you’ll innovate, collaborate, and thrive in a business that values your individuality and ambition. Join Luzern eCommerce and be part of the next big chapter in retail history! Tasks About the Role During a period of rapid growth, we are looking for a Marketplace Advertising Graduate to join our team, working across Amazon Sponsored Ads and DSP. This is an entry\-level role designed to provide structured training and hands\-on experience in digital advertising and marketplace management. You will work closely with experienced team members, supporting the delivery and optimisation of advertising campaigns while developing your technical and commercial skillset. You will gain exposure to the full account lifecycle, including campaign setup, performance analysis, reporting, and strategy, with ongoing support and mentorship from senior team members. This role offers excellent opportunities for learning, development, and long\-term career progression within a fast\-paced and supportive environment. What You’ll Be Doing * Support the creation and optimisation of Amazon Sponsored Ads campaigns * Assist in the setup and monitoring of Amazon DSP (programmatic) activity * Help prepare performance reports for clients on a regular basis * Analyse campaign and account performance with guidance from senior team members * Conduct research using internal and third\-party tools to identify growth opportunities * Support the wider team in delivering strong results across client accounts * Assist in preparing materials for client meetings and communications * Continuously learn about marketplace trends, tools, and best practices Training \& Development **You will receive structured training across:** * Amazon Advertising (Sponsored Ads \& DSP) * Campaign setup and optimisation * Data analysis and performance reporting * Marketplace and eCommerce fundamentals * Client communication and account management You will be supported throughout your development, with increasing responsibility as your skills grow. Requirements What You Bring to the Role Essential * Excellent written and verbal communication skills * Strong attention to detail * Good working knowledge of Excel and PowerPoint * Analytical mindset with the ability to interpret data * Strong organisational and time\-management skills * A proactive attitude and willingness to learn Beneficial (Not Required) * Academic or internship exposure to digital marketing, eCommerce, or data analysis * Interest in online marketplaces such as Amazon * Additional language skills (German, Spanish, French, or Italian) Benefits What We Offer * Competitive Compensation: Attractive salary package with benefits. * Work\-Life Balance: Competitive annual leave and flexible working arrangements. * Growth Opportunities: Access to learning and development programs to enhance your skills. * Culture and Environment: A fun, inclusive, and collaborative workplace. * Wellbeing Support: Access to an Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) * Quarterly Social Events: Company\-sponsored quarterly social events designed to bring teams together, celebrate success, and strengthen connections across the business We are committed to equal opportunities and welcome applications from all suitably qualified candidates. If you require any reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know.

title: Mechanical Design Engineer
description: Fohntech Group are currently looking to recruit Mechanical Design Engineer to join our team in Dublin with an immediate start. **Key Responsibilities of the Role:** * Collaborate with engineers, and construction managers to develop comprehensive project plans and ensure delivery on schedule. * Collaborate with any other consultants, subcontractors, supervisors, planners, estimators, quantity surveyors and the general workforce involved in the project. * Raise any design\-related risks to planning, commercial and management, and establish a course to minimise their impact. * Oversee the construction process to ensure adherence to design specifications and schedules. * Assist in developing design delivery schedules along with the client, project manager, design team and consultants. * Manage the deliverables and approvals processes of design packages to ensure full approval for procurement. * Check design programmes on projects are aligned to consultant resource and construction/ procurement requirements. * Ensure Mechanical technical compliance on all projects. * Assess and mitigate potential risks and issues as they’re identified in design, or that could have potential impact on construction activities. * Prepare detailed reports, and documentation for project approval and permits. * Provide technical guidance and support to construction teams. **Essential Criteria For The Role** * Minimum 2 years post graduate experience in the Industrial, Pharmaceutical, Life Sciences or Data Centre sectors. * Demonstrable design, technical and construction knowledge is essential for this role. **Next Steps** If you are interested in this role, please apply directly or email your CV to careers@fohntechgroup.com ### **Job Information** Date Opened 03/31/2026 Job Type Full time Industry Construction City Dublin 15 State/Province Dublin Country Ireland Zip/Postal Code D15

title: Mechanical/Electrical Engineer - 2+ yrs exp (Building Services)
description: **About Us:** Johnston Reid \& Associates is a leading Mechanical \& Electrical Consulting Engineering firm based in Sandyford Business Park (just off the M50\). We specialise in high\-end bespoke residential homes, hotels, healthcare facilities, retail spaces, and mixed\-use commercial developments. We’re looking for a **Junior Mechanical/Electrical Engineer** with at least **2 to 3** **years of experience** to join our growing team and contribute to exciting building services projects, including: * Bespoke Homes * Residential \& Commercial Developments * Offices \& Retail spaces * Hotels, pubs \& restaurants * Healthcare facilities **What You’ll Do:** * Support the design and installation of Mechanical \& Electrical services * Prepare detailed building services drawings using **Revit MEP** and **AutoCAD** * Attend sites for meetings and carry out inspections * Develop relationships with clients and contractors * Collaborate with senior engineers and the wider design team * Work across multiple projects simultaneously, including: **What We’re Looking For:** * 2\+ years in an engineering role (Building Services preferred) * Proactive, self\-motivated with ability to lead a team * Strong attention to detail, technical aptitude and critical thinking * **AutoCAD** and **Revit MEP** * Excellent communication and teamwork skills * This role requires regular travel to client sites (city centre \& regional). A full, clean driving licence and access to reliable transport is essential. **What We Offer:** * Competitive salary based on experience * Supportive and collaborative team environment * Exposure to diverse, high\-profile projects * Career development and on\-the\-job learning opportunities Job Type: Full\-time Experience: * a Mech/Elec Engineers Role (Building Services preferred): 2 years (preferred) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Microsoft SharePoint Architect
description: ### **Description** As we continue to experience rapid growth, we are seeking a highly skilled SharePoint Architect to join our team. The successful candidate will provide expert\-level support, administration, and consultancy across Microsoft SharePoint environments both on\-premises (SharePoint Server 2013/2016/2019 \& Subscription Edition) and SharePoint Online. The role combines advanced troubleshooting and incident resolution with the delivery of enhancements and change for our customers. Working closely with business stakeholders and technical teams, the consultant will ensure SharePoint solutions are optimised, secure, and aligned with business requirements. ### **Key Responsibilities** * Troubleshoot complex technical issues across SharePoint, Microsoft 365, Teams, and integrated systems; perform root cause analysis for recurring issues and implement permanent fixes. * Manage and maintain (SharePoint Server 2013/2016/2019 \& Subscription Edition), including site collections, permissions, content databases, and service applications. * Configure, monitor, and optimise performance, security, and availability. * Support migration projects (on\-premises to SharePoint Online, tenant\-to\-tenant, or version upgrades). * Develop and support custom solutions using SharePoint Framework (SPFx), TypeScript, React, and modern web technologies. * Build and maintain solutions using Power Platform (Power Automate, Power Apps, Power BI). ### **Skills, Knowledge and Expertise** **Required skills \& experience** * Minimum 5\-8 years' hands\-on experience with Microsoft SharePoint, including architecture, service applications, and administration. * SP data migrations (experience of Sharegate \- who also has good experience of PP/CE) * Proficiency across the Microsoft 365 ecosystem (Teams, OneDrive, Exchange Online). * Experience with PowerShell scripting for automation and troubleshooting. * Experience working within ticketing systems and SLA\-driven environments. **Desirable skills \& experience** * 5\+ years' experience with Power Platform technologies (Power Automate, Power Apps, Power BI). * Experience with migration tooling such as Sharegate, AvePoint Fly, or LinkFixer Advanced. * Experience with Teams Phone (Voice). ### **About Codec** Established over 40 years ago, Codec is a multi\-award\-winning Microsoft Solutions Partner, trusted by public and private sector organisations to deliver impactful digital transformation. With offices in **Dublin**, **Belfast**, **Cologne**, and across **Germany**, our team of over **300 experts** specialises in harnessing the full potential of Microsoft technologies—including **Dynamics 365**, **Power Platform**, **Azure**, and **Copilot**—to drive innovation, automation, and operational excellence. Codec combines deep industry expertise with a proven track record to help organisations unlock the power of AI and modern cloud solutions Though originally best known as a hardware reseller, since 2010 Codec has focused on building an outstanding software consulting business with a team of experts implementing enterprise solutions using products from Microsoft and Oracle. Our customer base, revenues and average contract values have grown steadily year on year and we continue to invest in the provision of new product and service offerings. Our team is a careful mix of recent graduates and experienced professionals from a variety of diverse cultural backgrounds. We are a melting pot of seasoned consultants supported by the best talent from the local universities. We have a thriving placement student academy and graduate recruitment programme which grows year on year. This company is an equal opportunities employer

title: Middle Office Associate - CLO Operations
description: **Company Overview** KKR is a leading global investment firm that offers alternative asset management as well as capital markets and insurance solutions. KKR aims to generate attractive investment returns by following a patient and disciplined investment approach, employing world\-class people, and supporting growth in its portfolio companies and communities. KKR sponsors investment funds that invest in private equity, credit and real assets and has strategic partners that manage hedge funds. KKR’s insurance subsidiaries offer retirement, life and reinsurance products under the management of Global Atlantic Financial Group. References to KKR’s investments may include the activities of its sponsored funds and insurance subsidiaries. Role Overview KKR is seeking to hire a Middle Office professional to join the Leveraged Credit Operations team in Dublin. The role supports a broad range of activities within the CLO Operations team, including, but not limited to, Asset Maintenance, Daily Trade Activity, MIS, Deal Closings, Trustee Reporting, and Portfolio Manager support. The successful candidate will also gain exposure to colleagues in our San Francisco, Gurugram, London, and New York offices. Key Responsibilities * Trustee Reporting * Payment Date Calculations * New Deal and Transaction Closings * Portfolio Manager Support * Management of relationships with third\-party Custodians and Administrators (US Bank, BNY Mellon, FIS Virtus) * Reviewing daily exception reporting * Ad hoc duties assisting other team members as required **Skills And Experience Required** * Demonstrated interest in the firm and the market, with a desire to learn about the firm and a broad range of investment products and activities * Ability to work on own initiative and demonstrate a proactive approach * Ability to work in a fast\-paced, challenging environment with changing priorities * Strong communication skills, both written and verbal * Strong academic background * Excellent interpersonal and teamwork skills, with the ability to work effectively with individuals at all levels of the organisation * Professionalism * Strong MS Excel skills with an analytical mindset * Prior CLO knowledge preferred * Prior experience with Advent Geneva and Allvue BMS Everest, or a similar product stack * A strong focus on improving processes and executing at scale KKR is an equal opportunity employer. Individuals seeking employment are considered without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, sexual orientation, or any other category protected by applicable law. KKR will provide reasonable accommodations as required by applicable federal, state, and/or local laws. Individuals seeking an accommodation for the application or interview process should email Benefits@kkr.com. Emails sent for unrelated issues, such as following up on an application, will not receive a response. title: Partner
description: Banking \& Finance Partner or Of Counsel A dynamic and growing mid\-tier commercial practice in Dublin is seeking an ambitious and highly accomplished **Banking \& Finance Partner** to join their busy and expanding Finance team. The successful candidate will be pivotal in leading sophisticated financing mandates, driving key business development initiatives across the Irish market, and mentoring junior solicitors. This role is ideal for an existing partner or a senior lawyer seeking a platform that offers direct impact, a clear path to equity, and the opportunity to shape the future of the department, focusing on both domestic lending and international financing structures into Ireland. Key Responsibilities * **Practice Leadership \& Execution** Lead and manage a diverse range of complex banking and finance transactions, leveraging expertise relevant to the Irish market: * **Corporate Lending:** Secured and unsecured bilateral and syndicated lending. * **Real Estate Finance:** Development and investment financing across commercial and residential sectors. * **Acquisition Finance:** Supporting M\&A and Private Equity\-led transactions. * **Debt Restructuring:** Advising on workouts, security enforcement, and corporate recovery. * **Regulatory Advisory:** Providing guidance on Irish financial services regulation and Central Bank of Ireland compliance. * **Oversee and draft** all necessary loan documentation, security suites, and legal opinions, ensuring commercial efficacy under Irish jurisdiction. **Business Development \& Client Management** * **Possess a portable client base** or a verifiable track record of significant business generation and a clear strategy for future growth within the Irish financial sector. * **Actively drive** the firm’s Finance Department's growth through targeted business development, attending industry events in Dublin, and leveraging professional networks (e.g., pillar banks, alternative lenders, and debt funds). * **Cultivate and maintain** strong relationships with existing and prospective clients, particularly domestic lenders, international financial institutions, and corporate borrowers. **Team Management \& Mentorship** * **Supervise, train, and mentor** junior lawyers and paralegals within the Banking team, ensuring their technical development in Irish finance law. * **Contribute** to the firm's strategic planning and management as a key member of the partnership. Qualifications * **Qualified Solicitor in Ireland** (or equivalent with an Irish practicing certificate and significant Irish law experience). * A minimum of **8\+ years Post\-Qualification Experience (PQE)** specializing in Banking \& Finance law, ideally gained within a reputable private practice setting in Dublin or a leading international firm. Technical Skills \& Experience * **Demonstrable expertise** in managing complex debt transactions, with proven knowledge of the Irish legal and regulatory framework. * **Deep understanding** of security structures, intercreditor dynamics, and the Irish Companies Act 2014 as it relates to corporate borrowing. * **Proven track record** of high\-quality legal drafting and sophisticated negotiation skills. * **Established connectivity** within the Dublin banking, private equity, and professional services community. Personal Attributes **Entrepreneurial Drive:** Highly motivated and proactive with a drive to generate and sustain a profitable practice in a competitive market. * **Commercial Acumen:** Ability to provide commercially focused, pragmatic, and risk\-aware advice to both lenders and borrowers. * **Leadership:** Strong leadership qualities with the ability to inspire, manage, and grow a team effectively. * Internal Description

title: Register your interest for our 2026 Digital Academy Programme
description: **Company Description** At Version 1, we believe in unlocking the potential of our Early Careers Associates. Our Digital Skills Academy has been revamped to provide cutting\-edge training on the latest technologies, empowering our associates to deliver high\-quality services to our customers. We have partnered with leading training providers to create bespoke academies that help our associates develop their IT skill sets and earn industry\-recognized certifications. The program's Early Careers framework enables you to build a strong network with peers across the business while advancing your career in technology. So far we have achieved some incredible accolades for our work: * Named 2023 Oracle EMEA Partner of the Year Award for Innovation * Named a Best Workplace™ UK in Tech by Great Place to Work® * Named a Best Workplace UK for Women by Great Place to Work® * Named India’s \#1 Mid\-Size Workplace by Great Place to Work India® * Named Global Microsoft Partner of the Year for Modernising Applications 2023 * Named a Best Workplace™ Ireland in Tech by Great Place to Work® * Awarded Best Project of the Year at the UKOUG Awards 2023 * Accredited with Investors in Diversity Silver mark by the Irish Centre for Diversity * Named a Best Super Large Workplace™ UK By Great Place to Work® * Named a Best Workplace™ India for Building a Culture of Innovation by All By Great Place to Work® * Named a Best Workplace™ Ireland for Women by Great Place to Work® * Named No. 1 Best Large Workplace™ Ireland \- 2023 By Great Place to Work® * Named a Best Workplace™ UK for Wellbeing 2023 By Great Place to Work® * Named a Top 10 Best Place To Work UK By Glassdoor *What can you help us achieve in 2024?* **Job Description** * PLEASE NOTE THAT THIS IS FOR REGISTERING YOUR INTEREST IN FUTURE ACADEMIES ACROSS ALL VERSION 1 LOCATIONS. THIS IS NOT A LIVE VACANCY AND YOU WILL BE CONTACTED WHEN AN ACADEMY BECOMES AVAILABLE IN YOUR HOME LOCATION (PLEASE ENSURE THAT UPON APPLICATION, YOU HAVE INCLUDED WHERE YOU ARE BASED/ YOUR HOME LOCATION TO HELP US DIRECT YOUR APPLICATION).\*\* - "Our goal in Version 1 is to ensure that a career in technology is accessible to everyone. That's why our Academy is open to college/university graduates, career changers, returners to the workforce, and individuals with technical skills who want to experience a culture of recognition and reward. We believe that everyone deserves a chance to learn, grow, and succeed in the technology industry. Our academies are bespoke and created with our associates in mind, we have created a specialised and supportive environment to provide you with the best possible start to your career within technology consultancy, with support from the Early Careers Team and accredited providers, you'll complete a 12\-week hybrid training academy, receive professional industry recognised certifications and accreditations as well as **receiving a highly competitive a training allowance as well as pre\-agreed salary uplifts to further motivate your development and progression.** * First 8\-weeks consists of general learning about different technologies and organisational concepts. You will meet Version 1 Mentors and Senior Technologists to learn how to practically use new skills in a Consultancy role. You will work with great trainers on your presentation and customer\-facing skills which will be part of the Design Thinking week. There will be week dedicated to the delivery of Business Analysis \& Project Management syllabus. The Academy includes interactive and fun events held by the Early Careers team, where you will have the opportunity to really get to know who we are and why we are so passionate about our Version 1 colleagues and Academy Associates. We pay for your certifications across our Partner Network: UK \& Ireland’s premier Microsoft, AWS \& Oracle partner, and many more… * The last 4\-weeks will specialise in a specific technology \- Java, .NET, DevOps, AWS Cloud etc * Upon graduating from our academy, we guarantee you an exclusive offer of permanent employment where you can put your new skills and qualifications into practice, working with our fantastic customer base on real\-world projects allowing you to begin your journey as a Version 1 difference maker. This is a once\-in\-a\-lifetime opportunity that you simply cannot afford to miss out on. Take the first step towards a successful career by enrolling in our academy today. * First 12\-weeks on Academy: paid training contract * Upon successful completion of 12\-week Academy: final interview with opportunity for Permanent Contract as an Associate Consultant * Over the first 18 months as Associate Consultant you will be eligible for salary uplifts (based on your performance): + 6 months – 5% increase + 12 months – 5% increase + 18 months – 10% increase **Qualifications** **Key Requirements:** **Our Digital Skills Academies Would Be Great If You** * Have received a university degree classification of 2:2 in any ICT or STEM subject * Love getting stuck into problem\-solving and you don’t give up easily! * Are passionate about technology * Eligible to work in the UK/ Ireland * Eligibility and willingness to travel across offices in the EU and UK to meet with our stakeholders and customers. (Travel Costs covered by Version 1\) * An added bonus if you have previous IT work experience or you participated in a Tech Bootcamp/course **Additional Information** **Version 1 is an equal opportunities employer.** We are committed to building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace where everyone feels valued and able to thrive. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities and lived experiences, and we value the different perspectives people bring. We want every candidate to have a positive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need reasonable adjustments at any stage of the process, please contact earlycareers@version1\.com at Version 1\. We will consider all requests carefully, respectfully and confidentially. If you have any questions please check out our dedicated Digital Skills Academy website: Version 1 Digital Skills Academy

title: Regulatory Affairs Associate
description: Home to the world's best loved and trusted hygiene, health, and nutrition brands. Our purpose defines why we exist: to protect, heal and nurture in the relentless pursuit of a cleaner, healthier world. We are a global team united by this purpose. Join us in our fight to make access to the highest quality hygiene, wellness, and nourishment a right and not a privilege.**Research and Development** ---------------------------- In Research and Development, we're full of highly skilled talents that include Scientists, Engineers, Medical, Clinical and Regulatory professionals \- all working to create a cleaner, healthier world. With nine Centres of Excellence, we continually seek out new opportunities by using science, our entrepreneurial flare and our fearless innovation to develop and enhance our existing portfolio, never compromising on quality or performance. We do the right thing, always, by ensuring we act with responsibility and integrity, by complying with regulatory legislation across the globe, whilst ensuring our products are safe for our consumers and are to the highest quality. The size of our organisation means you'll have the opportunity to learn and work in different functions within R\&D, giving you exposure to different disciplines, teams and environments. You will also have access to our Research and Development Academy, designed to develop our team and allow you to grow in our great organisation.**About the role** ------------------ As Part of the Regulatory Team \- You will work in the Regulatory function, to lead and undertake all regulatory work necessary to meet the needs of the Reckitt business in Ireland as per defined priorities set by regulatory management, and in particular to be responsible for reviewing and submitting variations in compliance with all local regulations and Reckitt internal procedures. **Your responsibilities** ------------------------- * Regulatory compliance for Ireland Marketing Authorisations in Reckitts’s portfolio, including preparation and submission of variation packages and renewals to the Regulatory Agency * New submissions/eCTD/NeeS * Extensive CMC experience for Ireland submission of type I and type II variations (including grouping and work\-sharing), such as site transfers, change of manufacturing processes, FSP and analytical methods * Gap analysis of quality dossiers for licensing, including experience in reviewing and quality check of CMC documents for electronic submission to HPRA * Submission of type II safety variations to update SmPC and labelling in line with Company Core Data Sheets * Working with PV on PSUR submissions and SmPC updates * Creation and assessment of internal quality and non\-quality changes in line with the change control management system * Artwork review and approvals (PIL, primary and secondary packaging), including preparation of User Test Bridging Reports for the approval of leaflet changes * Work collaboratively with the relevant country teams to establish and implement the regulatory strategies within agreed timescales to bring competitive advantage to Reckitt brands * Support UKI Drug Safety Officer and relevant qualified employees to ensure compliance with pharmacovigilance and quality obligations * Interpret and apply all relevant legislation in Ireland to keep the Reckitt business up to date on national regulatory trends, risks and opportunities * Provide regulatory support and advice to other functions to ensure products are maintained in compliance with the local requirements **The experience we're looking for** ------------------------------------ * University Science degree in relevant scientific discipline and regulatory experience within Consumer Health or OTC industry or FMCG * Thorough understanding of the current IE and European regulations, laws, guidelines and industry requirements * Exceptional ability to manage workload and identify priority tasks to meet tight deadlines * Strong influencing / communication expertise * Ability to build good relationships and challenge constructively and confidently * Ability to consolidate large volumes of complex information, interpret \& construct straightforward plans. Naturally inquisitive and interested in our brands * Confident and capable of operating at all levels and able to resolve conflict situations * Competent with PC tools and information systems * Ability to challenge the status quo and propose improvement * Able to apply appropriate problem\-solving techniques to evaluate and resolve operational issues **The skills for success** -------------------------- Commercial Awareness, Business Partnership, Regulatory, Product Lifecycle Management, Propose improvement, Accountability, Adaptability, Business Strategy, Project \& Time Management, Collaboration \& Cross‑Functional Partnership, Detail Orientation \& Accuracy **What we offer** ----------------- With inclusion at the heart of everything we do, working alongside our four global Employee Resource Groups, we support our people at every step of their career journey, helping them to succeed in their own individual way. We invest in the wellbeing of our people through parental benefits, an Employee Assistance Program to promote mental health, and life insurance for all employees globally. We have a range of other benefits in line with the local market. Through our global share plans we offer the opportunity to save and share in Reckitt's potential future successes. For eligible roles, we also offer short\-term incentives to recognise, appreciate and reward your work for delivering outstanding results. You will be rewarded in line with Reckitt's pay for performance philosophy.**Equality** ------------ We recognise that in real life, great people don't always 'tick all the boxes'. That's why we hire for potential as well as experience. Even if you don't meet every point on the job description, if this role and our company feels like a good fit for you, we still want to hear from you. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, disability or medical condition; colour, ethnicity, race, citizenship, and national origin; religion, faith; pregnancy, family status and caring responsibilities; sexual orientation; sex, gender identity, gender expression, and transgender identity; protected veteran status; size or any other basis protected by appropriate law.

title: Renewable Energy Engineer
description: **Project Engineer (Renewables)** Location: **Newbridge, Co. Kildare, Ireland** (Hybrid working model with blended **working from home** and on\-site where applicable) Duration: **Permanent** Reporting to: **PMO Program Manager** Our client is currently looking for a motivated and experienced **Project Engineer** to join their **Renewables team** in **Newbridge.** They are an **Irish Semi‑State Organisation** , headquartered in Newbridge, Co. Kildare, has evolved into a modern **Climate‑Action Enterprise** . It now focuses on **Renewable Energy Development** , rehabilitation, waste and resource recovery, and **Large‑Scale Environmental Restoration** projects. Its mission centres on supporting Ireland’s transition to a **Climate‑Neutral Economy** by **2050** through **Clean‑Energy Solutions, Biodiversity Regeneration** , and **Sustainable Land Management.** The Project Engineer with the **PMO** supports the delivery of their **Large\-Scale RenewableEnergy Infrastructure Projects** across all phases,from **Development** through to **Operation.** Working under the guidance of **Senior Project Managers** and the **PMO** , the role ensures project activities progress according to **Schedule, Budget** , and **Governance Standards** , contributing to the company’s **Low\-Carbon** and **RenewableEnergy** goals. **The Role:** The main duties and responsibilities of the role will include the following: **AssistSenior Project Managers** in **Planning, Coordinating** , and **Monitoring Project** activities across all phases.Maintain **Project Schedules** , action logs, and milestone trackingwithin the **PMO framework** . **Scope, Plan** and **Co\-Ordinate** activities on assigned work packages to successfully deliver required outputs. Ensure tasks assigned to functional teams are progressing as planned. Supportthe **Preparation, Review** , and **Administration Of Contracts, Purchase Orders** , and procurement documents. Help track project budgets, expenditures, and financial reporting under the oversight of **Senior Project Managers** . Contributeto maintaining accurate project documentation and financial records. Support the team in tracking **Planning Consents, Permits** , and **Regulatory Approvals** required for **Construction** and **Operation** . Ensuredocumentation is maintained to demonstrate compliance with regulatory and environmental requirements. Assistin maintaining effective communication with internal teams, advisors, suppliers, and externalstakeholders. Helpprepare reports, presentations, and updates for **PMO, Senior Project Managers** , and **Program Managers** . Participate in projectmeetings and followup on assigned actions. Supportrisk identification, monitoring, and reporting under the guidance of **Senior Project Managers.** Assistin preparing operational handover documentation to support readiness for **Project Commissioning.** Maintain accurate projectrecords and dashboards to support **PMOreporting** . Contribute to project governance by following **PMO methodologies** and processes. Capture lessons learned and share observations to improve projectdelivery practices. All employeesare expected to co\-operate fully with all provisions taken by the company for ensuring Health and Safety, and Welfare of co\-employees and members of the public using the premises Proactively participate in the **Performance Management process** to ensure delivery of own, and the wider team's,objectives. **The Person:** The ideal candidate will possess the following qualification, skills, knowledge, and attributes: A third\-level degree in **Engineering, Business, Project Management** or a related discipline (Master’s degree advantageous). **Minimum 3 years’ experience in Large Scale Energy** or **Infrastructure Project Delivery** Competency across a broad range **Project Management** skills must be demonstrated. Familiarity with **Energy Infrastructure** sector in **Ireland.** Excellent **Technical Appraisal, Report Writing** and communications skills essential. **Professional Project Management Certification** (e.g. **PMP** or equivalent) is desirable. Proficient in **MS Office** and **MS Project.** Post graduate qualification in **Project Management** is desirable **Other Benefits Include:** Employer matched pension contributions They invest in their people and have a strong commitment to your development. They provide free, confidential counselling \& wellbeing support service that provides in\-the\-moment support to you. It also provides access to self\-guided meditation, Fitness \& Nutrition Pathways, Digital Gym \& on demand webinars. They fund subscriptions for work\-related professional bodies. Interested? Please send a copy of your CV to **hello@enexa\-partners.com**

title: Reporting and Accounting Officer - based in Luxembourg
description: **This position is based at our Luxembourg headquarters and requires regular office presence.** The EIB offers you the opportunity to live and work in a truly international and multi\-cultural environment. We also offer relocation support. The **EIB** , the European Union's bank, is seeking to recruit for its Chief Financial Controller Directorate (CFC), Financial Reporting and Accounting Department (FRA), Corporate Accounting and Reporting Division (CAR), Accounting, Reporting \& Payments Unit (ARP), at its headquarters in Luxembourg, a **Reporting and Accounting Officer\*.** **This is a full\-time position at grade 5 for which the EIB offers a permanent contract.** * Internal benchmark Officer Financial Accounting ***Panel interviews are anticipated for May/June.*** Purpose As a Reporting and Accounting Officer, you will contribute to the unit’s work in evaluating the accounting impacts for administrative expenses and fee income in view of formulating recommendations on process optimisations as well as on the application of accounting policies, whilst also carrying out regular controls, preparing analytical reporting aiming to ensure the accuracy of the EIB Group financial statements within the framework of EU Directives and International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS). Operating Network You will be reporting directly to the Head of the Accounting, Reporting and Payments Unit (ARP) within the Corporate Accounting Division. Accountabilities * Hold responsibility for executing accounting, control and reporting processes in operating and capital expenses and fee income. * Deliver on payment processes for administrative expenses including HR related transactions. * Execute regular reviews and evaluations of business processes. Update of their procedure manuals and the related Internal Control Framework. * Contribute to the communication of accounting, reporting and payment related strategies, policies and procedures throughout the organisation to ensure an efficient delivery of the unit’s responsibilities. * Contribute to digital transformation projects and cross\-directorate process improvements to align the Bank’s processes and policies to best practice. * Participate in the communication and the implementation of best\-in\-class practices and policies throughout the Bank and within the own directorate to ensure an efficient delivery of the unit’s responsibilities. * Maintain external and internal relations relating to accounting and financial reporting to enable the Bank to comply with applicable accounting standards and apply best in class practices and policies. * Provide guidance and support to more junior staff. **Qualifications** * University degree (minimum an equivalent to a Bachelor) preferably in Business Administration, Accounting, Finance or related fields * At least 5 years of relevant professional experience, ideally in a similar role in accounting, finance or related areas in a multinational organization or a major audit firm. * Sound knowledge of International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) and European Directives on accounting standards. * Experience in the preparation of analytical financial reporting and policies. * An internationally recognized professional accounting qualification (like ACCA, ACA, CIMA, etc.) would be an advantage. * Knowledge of payment tools and processes would be an advantage. * Knowledge of software applications such as PeopleSoft Financials (GL, AP, AR), Business Objects, Wall Street Suite (TRM, CLM), Certent Disclosure Management will be an asset. * Excellent knowledge of English and/or French (\*\*\*) with a good command of the other. Knowledge of other EU languages would be an advantage. Competencies **About** *Find out more about EIB core behavioural skills* here To find out more about our eligibility criteria click here *(\*\*\*) Unless stated explicitly as a required qualification, a good command of French is not a pre\-requisite for hire. As both English and French are however official working languages of the EIB, proficiency in both languages is a pre\-requisite for your future career development. Any language clause in your contract must be fulfilled in order for you to be eligible for a promotion (either via the annual appraisal cycle or via an internal selection process). Proficiency is understood to mean the attainment of level 5 of the Inter Institutional language courses, corresponding to B1\.2 of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFRL). The Bank offers appropriate training support.* We hire and value talent with unique characteristics, creating a work environment where they can be themselves. We believe that Diversity, Equity and Inclusion makes us a performing and innovative organisation. We encourage all suitably qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity/expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity. If you require reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process due to a disability, neurodivergence, or a chronic health condition, please contact the EIB Recruitment team Jobs@eib.org who will manage your request appropriately. By applying for this position, you acknowledge the importance of maintaining the security and integrity of the Information of the EIB Group. In case of selection for the position you agree to comply with all measures (policies, controls, document classification and management) implemented by the EIB Group to prevent unauthorised disclosure of any information or any damage to the EIB Group reputation. Deadline for applications: **2nd April 2026**

title: Retail Sales @ Brand Central
description: We are seeking a dedicated Retail Sales Associates to join our team at our new store BRAND CENTRAL in Fairgreen Shopping Centre Carlow. In this role, you will play a vital part in delivering outstanding service to our customers, contributing to our mission of excellent care and support. This role will be starting immediately . **Responsibilities** * Assist customers with their inquiries and provide product recommendations. * Maintain a clean and organized sales floor to enhance the shopping experience. * Process transactions accurately and efficiently at the cash register. * Collaborate with team members to achieve sales goals and improve customer satisfaction. * Stay informed about product offerings and promotions to effectively assist customers. * Handle customer complaints professionally and resolve issues promptly. * Participate in training sessions to enhance product knowledge and sales techniques. * **Qualifications** * Previous experience in retail or customer service is preferred. * Strong communication skills with the ability to engage effectively with customers. * Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment. title: Revenue Operations Analyst
description: **The Company** PayPal has been revolutionizing commerce globally for more than 25 years. Creating innovative experiences that make moving money, selling, and shopping simple, personalized, and secure, PayPal empowers consumers and businesses in approximately 200 markets to join and thrive in the global economy. We operate a global, two\-sided network at scale that connects hundreds of millions of merchants and consumers. We help merchants and consumers connect, transact, and complete payments, whether they are online or in person. PayPal is more than a connection to third\-party payment networks. We provide proprietary payment solutions accepted by merchants that enable the completion of payments on our platform on behalf of our customers. We offer our customers the flexibility to use their accounts to purchase and receive payments for goods and services, as well as the ability to transfer and withdraw funds. We enable consumers to exchange funds more safely with merchants using a variety of funding sources, which may include a bank account, a PayPal or Venmo account balance, PayPal and Venmo branded credit products, a credit card, a debit card, certain cryptocurrencies, or other stored value products such as gift cards, and eligible credit card rewards. Our PayPal, Venmo, and Xoom products also make it safer and simpler for friends and family to transfer funds to each other. We offer merchants an end\-to\-end payments solution that provides authorization and settlement capabilities, as well as instant access to funds and payouts. We also help merchants connect with their customers, process exchanges and returns, and manage risk. We enable consumers to engage in cross\-border shopping and merchants to extend their global reach while reducing the complexity and friction involved in enabling cross\-border trade. Our beliefs are the foundation for how we conduct business every day. We live each day guided by our core values of Inclusion, Innovation, Collaboration, and Wellness. Together, our values ensure that we work together as one global team with our customers at the center of everything we do – and they push us to ensure we take care of ourselves, each other, and our communities. **Job Description Summary:** What you need to know about the role: As part of PayPal’s Europe Revenue Operations team, we’re seeking a curious, data\-driven problem solver to turn insights into actions that drive commercial performance. This role sits at the heart of the revenue engine, partnering with Sales, Account Management, and leadership to improve pipeline visibility, optimise processes, and enable faster, smarter decisions. If you enjoy solving complex problems and using data to unlock growth, this role is for you. Meet our team: We partner with Sales, Account Management, Finance, Marketing, and Product to drive growth and efficiency—acting as strategic advisors to leadership. We ensure clear performance visibility, scalable processes, and high\-quality data to inform decisions. Why this role: Work at the intersection of data, strategy, and execution—driving impact, gaining senior exposure, and building a strong foundation for future growth. **Job Description:** **Essential Responsibilities:** * Analyze sales data to recommend process improvements. * Collaborate with internal teams to enhance operational efficiency. * Prepare detailed reports for management review. * Handle complex customer inquiries and provide solutions. * Mentor junior analysts and guide their professional growth. **Minimum Qualifications:** * 3\+ years relevant experience and a Bachelor’s degree OR Any equivalent combination of education and experience. **Additional Responsibilities And Preferred Qualifications** **Your way to impact** You will directly influence revenue performance by: * Improving pipeline health, forecasting accuracy, and sales efficiency * Identifying growth opportunities and operational bottlenecks through structured, critical thinking * Leading and coordinating cross\-functional initiatives that improve how our revenue engine operates * Enabling leadership to make faster, data\-driven decisions This is a high\-impact, highly visible role with direct exposure to senior stakeholders. **Your day\-to\-day** Insights \& Performance * Analyze sales and customer data to uncover insights on performance, win/loss trends, and revenue drivers * Apply structured problem\-solving and critical thinking to identify root causes and recommend actions * Define and track key metrics (pipeline velocity, quota attainment, conversion rates) * Build and maintain dashboards to enable real\-time visibility and decision\-making Forecasting \& Pipeline Management * Partner with sales leadership to run pipeline reviews and forecasting discussions * Use data and judgment to challenge assumptions, identify risks, and improve forecast accuracy * Own pipeline quality end\-to\-end—ensuring strong visibility, hygiene, and data integrity in Salesforce to enable reliable forecasting and decision\-making Process, Tools \& Program Management * Own and drive initiatives to improve sales processes and workflows, from identifying problems to delivering solutions * Lead cross\-functional programs, ensuring alignment and effective execution across teams * Identify opportunities to simplify and scale how the commercial organisation operates * Drive adoption of tools, processes, and best practices to improve sales efficiency and consistency Annual Planning * Support and help operationalise the annual planning process, including market
